recipes.title: Garlic Shrimp Zucchini Noodle Recipe
ingredients: 2 Medium zucchinis or 1 package spiralized zucchini
3 tablespoons goat cheese, crumbled or feta cheese
instructions: Spiralize your zucchini or set aside a package of store-bought spiral zucchini. Remove the Trifecta Shrimp from the container and rinse, set aside. For the garlic use a Microplane or grater instead of trying to cut it up with your knife. This is a great trick when a recipe calls for a lot of garlic! Measure out all of the additional ingredients, feel free to add additional vegetables or more garlic depending on what you like!

Heat a non-stick skillet or saute pan over medium heat and add 1 teaspoon avocado oil and garlic. Cook for 2 minutes or until aromatic, add in the Trifecta shrimp and cook for 2 more minutes. If you are using raw shrimp, cook for 6-8 minutes until opaque and cooked through.
Add Acids, Herbs, and Goat Cheese
Add in the tomatoes and zucchini noodles, cook for 1-2 minutes until noodles are coated with the sauce.
recipes.summary: Prepare this creamy garlic shrimp zucchini noodle recipe in less than 10 minutes for a quick low-carb noodle dish.

recipes.title: Chicken and Mushroom Sauced-Noodle Recipe
ingredients: 8 oz cooked Lo mein, ramen, or lamian noodles
Cilantro or Thai basil for garnish, optional
instructions: Cook the noodles of your choice according to the packaging instructions. Drain, rinse under cold water and return to the same pan. Keep warm on the side off the heat. I highly encourage you to cook your noodles al dente -- this will help offset any potential reheating you may do.

If you so desire, add the hoisin and Sriracha to a small saucepan. Conversely, do not add these two optional ingredients, and simply add the Thai-peanut sauce of your choice to a pan and begin reducing it while all the other ingredients cook. I suggest you start this process as you are dropping your noodles to cook since you want a nicely reduced sauce to coat all your noodles evenly.

In a large saute pan, add the oil and bring the heat up to medium-high. Once the oil is shimmering, cook the garlic for a quick 1 minute. Immediately add the chicken, and cook for 2 minutes, stirring often. Add a pinch of lime juice to deglaze the pan if the bottom is getting too brown. Add the mushroom, saute and then add the spinach. Stir often to help cook the spinach and continue to reheat all other items. Lastly, I also suggest adding a splash of the sauce to the pan to coat all of the items and flavor them further. Taste for seasoning and if needed, add a pinch of salt.

Combine the now reduced sauce with the noodles and bring to a light simmer to reheat the noodles and sauce them. Add a pinch of water to the sauce if it has reduced too much. The sauce should beautifully coat the noodles - if the sauce is too watery, reduce the sauce a bit further with the noodles in it. Avoid reducing the sauce excessively or cooking the noodles in the sauce for too long as this will overcook them.

In a bowl, combine 3 ounces of the sauced noodles, along with 5 ounces of the chicken-vegetable mix. Mix to incorporate. Be careful not to burn yourself. Transfer to a plate and serve. Add any extra vegetables as desired for color and extra fiber (I added cherry tomatoes).
recipes.summary: This chicken and mushroom sauced-noodle recipe is a heart-warming peanut-Thai based recipe that will satisfy any weekday meal prep need.

recipes.title: Easy Sweet and Sour Chicken Recipe
ingredients: 1 tablespoon grapeseed oil (or any neutral tasting oil)
instructions: Remove Trifecta chicken from the container and dice to bite-size pieces, as even as possible. Measure Trifecta broccoli, asparagus, and brown rice and hold on the side. Measure garlic and grapeseed oil into individual containers, similar to the sweet and sour sauce.

In a large saute or frying pan, heat the oil and over medium to medium-low heat, saute the garlic stirring often to avoid the garlic from burning. Season with salt and increase the heat to medium-high. Add the chicken. Warm chicken completely through about 1-2 minutes. Adjust heat as needed (lower at this point to medium) and add half of the sweet and sour. Scrape the bottom of the pan for brown bits, as adding the sauce will deglaze the pan.

Add the rice first, as grains take a little longer to warm through. Adjust heat to medium and mix often for about 1-2 minutes. Remove the chicken and rice mix into a holding bowl. Keep warm in a countertop oven.
Plate the sweet and sour chicken by laying the vegetables down, and then plating the chicken and rice on top. Spread the remaining sweet and sour sauce on top of your dish or meal prep. Enjoy!
recipes.summary: This easy sweet and sour chicken recipe is a quick meal that can be prepared in10 minutes or less!

recipes.title: Chilaquiles Chicken Recipe
ingredients: are basically partially baked nachos drenched in salsa.
The basic chilaquiles equation: tortillas drenched in sauce, mixed with cheese, and topped with any kind of protein and toppings of your choice.
If you want to make it vegan and high-protein, use some soyrizo or
, or if you want to add some protein and a sunny side-up egg, you can definitely do that too!
to prepare this simple and delicious
into the mix to help you round up this meal; a few ideas are roasted peppers, green or red (think poblano), if desired sautéed spinach, and maybe even cherry tomatoes.
You can also finish your chilaquiles with queso fresco or Cotija cheese, a chunky salsa, and some chopped avocado.
This recipe calls for 18 chips per serving size. I suggest using half of that to drench them in the salsa, and the other half to be used as a base and crunch factor on the plate.
Top your finished product with fresh herbs like cilantro or parsley for a perfect Instagram brunch-at-home picture!
instructions: Measure chips, remove Trifecta shredded chicken from the container, cut the avocado and liberally cover with lime juice and a pinch of salt, crumble the cotija, and grab your oil. Having your ingredients at hand will allow for a very fast set up.

Heat the oil over medium heat until oil is shimmering. Carefully add the salsa of choice and cook 3-5 minutes. Adjust heat as needed. If your salsa is chunky, feel free to blend it using a hand stick blender.

Add the tortillas into the pan and mix very gently to avoid breaking the chips. Because the quantity of chips is so large, I suggest you do this in batches to avoid having a difficult time mixing the salsa with the tortilla chips. The chips should be well soaked. Add the chicken, mix once more and keep on heat for 1-2 minutes, until the chicken is warmed through.

Plate by either serving half of the tortilla chips on the plate and then topping off with the tortilla, salsa, chicken mix, and then garnish with seasoned avocado, cilantro stems, and cotija cheese.Enjoy immediately.
If desired, carefully blend the salsa either after cooking it in the oil in the frypan or before adding to the pan in a separate bowl using an immersion blender.
recipes.summary: This red chilaquiles chicken recipe is a fun brunch or taco Tuesday-ish recipe to satisfy any Mexican cuisine cravings while sticking to your macros.

recipes.title: Paleo Roasted Spiced Nuts Recipe
instructions: Preheat your oven to 350F. In a bowl, measure both cups of cashew and almonds. Roast for 6 minutes. Remove from the oven and transfer slightly roasted nuts to a bowl. Decrease oven temperature to 300F.
In a separate bowl, whip egg whites to soft peaks. Meanwhile, gently melt the butter in a small saucepan. We used our lovely Breville Smart Oven.
Pour melted butter first, followed by maple syrup and chile powder, cinnamon powder, ginger powder, nutmeg, kosher salt, and cayenne pepper on top of the roasted nuts.
Slowly add the and whipped egg whites into the bowl, folding with a rubber spatula gently.
Transfer now-seasoned nuts to a lined baking sheet. Spread out evenly.

Bake nuts for 16 minutes. Set a timer for 8 and rotate the baking pan halfway through. Set a timer for an additional 8 minutes. Once the time is up, remove from oven, let cool to room temperature, and store in an airtight container. Enjoy 1 oz servings.
recipes.summary: A simple recipe for creating any kind of delicious paleo-friendly spiced nuts, a perfect snack for your meal prep!

recipes.title: Paleo Broccoli Crispy Tots Recipe
instructions: Prepare all your ingredients. If purchased whole, cut cauliflower and broccoli into individual florets. Bring a big pot of water to a boil and salt liberally to achieve the same saltiness level as ocean water. Grab a separate bowl and fill it with ice and water to shock the vegetables. This will stop the cooking process and will avoid the vegetables from overcooking.

Once water is at a full rolling boil, blanch the cauliflower and broccoli in batches, always keeping the water boiling and avoiding it from cooling down too much. Using a spider or a large slotted spoon, remove the vegetables from the water into the iced water and submerge completely. Repeat this process until all cauliflower and broccoli are blanched.

Remove the vegetables from the ice water and pat dry with paper towels, removing as much excess water as possible. Transfer vegetables to a food processor and process them until broccoli and cauliflower are almost like a paste-like consistency. Transfer to a medium-sized bowl.

Preheat air fryer to french fries setting. In the same bowl, add coconut flour, garlic powder, coriander powder, paprika, cayenne pepper, and salt. Mix to fully incorporate, making sure all ingredients are mixed together into a uniform consistency. Using a small ice cream scoop, scoop the mixture into a parchment-lined sheet pan. Line them up keeping some distance in between.
Using a small ice cream scoop, scoop the mixture into a parchment-lined sheet pan. Line them up keeping some distance in between.

Place tots into the air fryer tray. Air-fry according to instructions, but generally, these tots will crips up and cook anywhere from around 10 to 15 minutes. Remove from the air-fryer and cool down. Serve with favorite sauces and eat as a snack throughout your week.
recipes.summary: A highly delicious and healthy crispy paleo-approved tot recipe made with broccoli to satisfy your weekly snack needs!

recipes.title: Paleo Coconut Yogurt Parfait Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups plain coconut yogurt (Plain So Delicious was used in this recipe)
Cinnamon powder and fresh mint leaves for garnish
instructions: If you haven’t already, prepare Trifecta’s paleo gluten-free granola recipe, or gather your paleo granola of choice.

Grab four separate small containers. Per container portion ¼ cup of coconut yogurt, ¼ cup of the paleo gluten-free granola, ¼ cup of mixed berries and sprinkle cinnamon powder on top of each container. Serve immediately or store in fridge. Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Our paleo parfait recipe uses coconut yogurt as a base, with this exciting paleo granola, and a mix of your favorite fruit to create a delicious snack!

recipes.title: Gluten Free Protein Waffle Recipe
ingredients: ½ cup whey protein powder of choice, we used
¼ cup milk or almond milk
instructions: Preheat your waffle maker and then measure out all of the ingredients into two bowls: eggs, milk, vanilla, in one and then all of the dry ingredients in another.
Place all of the wet ingredients into a blender and blend on high for 30 seconds, then add the dry ingredients and blend for 1-2 minutes until thoroughly mixed.

Once your waffle maker has heated up, spray each side with non-stick cooking spray or coconut oil. Measure 1/4 cup of batter for each waffle and cook for 1 1/2 - 2 minutes until golden brown, these waffles cook fast! Once they have finished cooking, dig in!
recipes.summary: Our gluten-free protein waffles recipe is packed with 18g of protein per waffle and can be prepared in less than 15 minutes.

recipes.title: Paleo Gluten-Free Granola Recipe
instructions: Preheat oven to 300F. Measure cashews, macadamia nuts and almonds into a food processor. Pulse until the nuts are rough chopped, 3-5 times. Transfer to a bowl. In the same bowl, add pepitas chia seeds, crancherry, coconut flakes, cinnamon, allspice, and cayenne pepper.
In a separate bowl, combine melted or liquid coconut oil, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and almond flour. Mix to combine.
Combine nut/seed mix with coconut oil/almond flour mix. Mix to fully coat all ingredients together.

Transfer paleo granola to a parchment-lined baking sheet and spread evenly. Bake for 20 minutes in a preheated oven. Remove from the oven after time is up, let cool completely and then break into small pieces. Transfer to an airtight container.
recipes.summary: This succulent, nutty, and texture-rich granola is an incredible paleo-approved and gluten-free granola recipe you can make in bulk and use as needed.

recipes.title: Paleo Breakfast Sausage Casserole Recipe
ingredients: 6 scallions, white bulbs sliced, greens reserved for garnish
½ lbs white potato, diced small
instructions: Preheat oven to 375F. Dice the potatoes into small even squares. Transfer to a foil-lined baking sheet and season with 2 teaspoons of oil, dried thyme, dried mustard powder, salt, and pepper. Bake for 20 minutes until the potatoes are just cooked through. Remove from the oven and transfer to a shallow baking dish. Evenly layer the potatoes in the bottom of the baking dish and keep on the side until needed.

Meanwhile, thinly slice the white bulb scallions, reserving the green bulbs. Transfer the sliced scallions to a bowl and combine it with the minced garlic. Reheat the remaining 1 teaspoon of oil in a pan and saute until translucent. Season with salt and pepper. Remove from heat and let cool. As scallion-garlic mix cooks process the sausages in a food processor until crumbled. Avoid overprocessing too thinly. Add in the same pan as the scallion and garlic mix.
Crack 10 eggs into a bowl and whisk to fully incorporate. Season with fennel seeds, salt, pepper and combine with the scallion, garlic, and sausage mix.
Pour egg mix on top of the potato in the baking dish. Transfer the shallow baking dish to the preheated oven and bake for 30 minutes.

Check constantly and add time as needed if the eggs are still runny. Poke a cake tester, toothpick, or fork in the middle to test doneness. The toothpick should come out clean with no eggs sticking to it when done.

Remove from oven and let cool for a few minutes. Using an offset spatula, loosen the casserole from the pan. Carefully and swiftly transfer out into a cutting board and portion into 8 squares. Cool down uncovered and portion each square to about 3.3 oz or 88g. Slice the green scallions thinly and garnish if desired.
recipes.summary: A paleo casserole recipe is an easy meal prep or family-style cooking dish that can feed many mouths or create several meals for your weekly meal prep.

recipes.title: Paleo Shakshuka Breakfast Recipe
ingredients: 1 red bell pepper, cored and seeded, sliced lengthwise
instructions: Preheat oven to 375 F. Slice onions lengthwise and transfer to a small bowl. Slice the bell peppers in a similar fashion, taking the core out and discarding all seeds. Mince garlic if needed or portion if you have store-bought minced garlic. Prepare all spices in a small separate bowl and keep aside until needed. Use an immersion blender to break down any larger chunks of the can crushed tomatoes.

Heat up 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a deep pan or a Dutch oven, over medium heat. Saute the onions for 3-5 minutes adjusting the heat to medium or medium-high, until these are fully translucent. Add bell peppers and saute for another 2-3 minutes. Add the garlic, saute for 1 minute. Season with salt, black pepper, paprika, cayenne pepper, and cumin. Mix to incorporate.

Immediately add the can crushed tomatoes to the pan. Mix to incorporate, scraping brown bits. Add 1 dash of tabasco, plus 1 tablespoon of maple syrup and simmer for 10 minutes until tomato mix thickens up.
Crack 4 eggs inside the dutch oven and lightly season with salt and pepper. Transfer the dutch oven to the oven and bake for 8 minutes to set the eggs.

After the time is up, remove the pan from the oven, let cool completely and transfer 1 egg per container along with a healthy dose of the Shakshuka tomato-base. Serve with a side of vegetables, or any favorite paleo baked good.
recipes.summary: Shakshuka is a recipe based on a tomato, herb, and pepper stew-like base topped with eggs that are cooked in the same stew’s pan. Enjoy our paleo version!

recipes.title: Paleo Sweet Potato Hash with Sausage & Egg Recipe
ingredients: 5 cups sweet potato, diced, roasted (about 3 medium-sized potatoes)
1 cup onions, yellow, small diced, sautéed
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400F (you can use a countertop oven). Begin by cutting your sweet potato and onions. Make sure you wash your sweet potato before cutting. Dice as evenly as possible and keep aside in a bowl. After you are done cutting your sweet potato, dice your onions in a similar size as the sweet potato. Transfer onions to a separate bowl and keep aside until needed.

Quickly wash Brussels sprouts on a colander and cut off stems. Discard stems, and then quarter the Brussels sprouts. Transfer to a third separate bowl and season with salt and pepper, 2 teaspoons of avocado oil, and mix with hands to evenly spread the seasoning. Slice the sausages in circles and add these to the same sheet pan like the one you will use to cook the Brussels sprouts. Keep the sausage and the Brussel sprouts separated but evenly distributed.

Season sweet potatoes in the same bowl with salt and pepper, 2 teaspoon paprika, 1 teaspoon thyme, zest from 1 orange, and 2 teaspoon of avocado oil. Mix with your hands to season evenly. Using different sheet trays, transfer the sweet potato to a foil lined sheet tray and spread to avoid having sweet potatoes overlapping.

Bake all sheet trays at the same time. The Brussels sprouts may take a slightly shorter amount of time than the sweet potato. Roast the Brussels sprouts and sausage for 13 minutes and the sweet potato for 25 minutes. Once done, there shouldn’t be any resistance to the sweet potatoes when poked with a fork.

Meanwhile, heat a saute pan with the remaining 2 teaspoons of avocado oil. Saute onions over medium heat, adjusting heat as needed, until caramelized for 5-6 minutes, mixing constantly. Deglaze the pan if the bottom starts to brown with a splash of water. This will help caramelize the onions further. Only season with salt and pepper before removing from the pan. Transfer to a bowl and cool down once ready. The further caramelized these are, the more flavor they will have.

Once all the ingredients are cooled down, divide ingredients evenly into four containers. For exact macros, portion 1 cup of sweet potatoes, ¼ cup onions, topped by ¼ cup of Brussels sprouts, and ¼ cup of the baked sausage. Top each container with 1 egg.
recipes.summary: Our well-balanced and macro-oriented paleo sweet potato hash with sausage & egg Recipe is easy to prepare ahead of time and enjoy every morning.

recipes.title: Paleo Porridge with Caramelized Bananas Recipe
instructions: Combine in a container large enough to hold 2 cups of nuts plus water, the cashews and walnuts completely covered with water. Cover with plastic wrap or a lid and soak overnight or for 8 hours in the refrigerator.

After the nuts have been soaked overnight, drain the water completely, and transfer the nuts to a food processor. Add the chia seeds, coconut flakes, pitted dates and process until the mix is finely chopped, stopping just before the nuts becomes a nut butter. Scrape the sides as needed.

Transfer the now ‘paleo porridge’ mix to a sauce pan, and combine with 2 cups of coconut milk, and 1 tablespoon of maple syrup. Bring slowly to a boil over medium-heat mixing constantly. Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Caramelize Bananas
Transfer the paleo porridge to three bowls or containers and plate each quarter banana on top of the porridge. Sprinkle each with some cinnamon powder and enjoy!
recipes.summary: This highly nutritious breakfast paleo porridge recipe can be eaten hot or cold, for any meal or snack of the day and packs a punch of flavor for you!

recipes.title: Paleo Applesauce Protein Pancakes Recipe
instructions: In a bowl whisk together all 6 eggs. Add half of the apple sauce, whisk until fully incorporated and then add the second half of the apple sauce along with the lemon zest. Whisk to incorporate. Add the maple syrup and whisk once more.
In a separate bowl mix the almond flour, baking soda and powder, and the cinnamon powder.
Combine the wet ingredients into the dry, mixing to fully incorporate. Whisk until a batter is formed.

Heat up over medium-low heat a non-stick large pan, or a portable flat top. Spray the surface area of the pan with cooking spray. Scoop ¼ cup per pancake and cook 1.5 to 2 minutes per side, flipping the pancakes using a wooden or plastic spatula. Avoid overcooking each side and adjust the heat as needed. Repeat the process until all the batter is cooked off.

Serve pancakes with maple syrup, and a healthy cup of a berry mix and some freshly picked mint for freshness and color. If meal prepping, cool down pancakes on a cooling rack as you cook them and store in a ziploc bag in the refrigerator or freezer.
recipes.summary: This recipe will help you make incredibly fluffy, highly delicious, and healthy paleo and gluten-free pancakes for you to enjoy as part of your meal prep!

recipes.title: Paleo Pumpkin Spice Protein Smoothie Recipe
instructions: Measure all ingredients and combine them together into a blender.

Blend until fully combined for about 45 seconds to 1 minute. Use any smoothie setting that your blender may have. Conversely, slowly increase the speed of your blender to avoid the lid from aggressively exploding.
Measure each portion of the smoothie to be 8 fluid ounces, topping off each glass with any excess amount left. If desired, sprinkle each with a small amount of cinnamon.
recipes.summary: A quick and easy delicious macro-balanced paleo pumpkin spice protein smoothie recipe that great for your meal prep.

recipes.title: Healthy Mashed Sweet Potato Recipe
instructions: Preheat your oven to 425 degrees. Rinse off the sweet potatoes and leave the skin on, then dice into large chunks (1” x 1”) and place them into a deep-sided baking dish.
Add the coconut milk and any spices you want to use to flavor your roasted sweet potato mash. Feel free to substitute other dairy-free milk alternatives instead of coconut milk.
Cover the dish with foil and bake for 25 minutes, until sweet potatoes are fork-tender.

Carefully remove the dish from the oven and mash with a potato masher or use an immersion blender for a smoother texture, we used our hand-held blender Breville all-in-one for a quick blend and clean-up! Once blended, season additionally with extra spices, lemon juice, or apple cider vinegar.
recipes.summary: You'll love this dairy-free sweet potato mash recipe; achieve organic sweetness and a velvety texture with just two simple ingredients.

recipes.title: High Protein Vegan Fudge Brownie Recipe
ingredients: 1 ¼ cup protein powder (vanilla or chocolate)
2 teaspoon vanilla extract or almond extract
instructions: In a large bowl mix cacao powder, protein powder, almond flour, baking powder, and salt. You can use a vanilla or chocolate protein powder depending on how chocolatey you like your brownies.
In a small bowl combine the maple syrup, coconut oil, vanilla extract and dairy free milk until combined.
Blend together the wet and dry ingredients with a whisk or spatula until uniformly mixed. Divide batter evenly into a muffin tin sprayed with non-stick cooking spray. Bake for 22 minutes.
To prepare a quick vegan chocolate ganache, combine the cacao powder, coconut oil, vanilla extract, salt, maple syrup and dairy-free milk in a small bowl until smooth.
recipes.summary: A drool-worthy high protein vegan fudge protein brownie recipe for a perfect chocolatey, rich, gooey-fudgy center, and 13 grams of protein per brownie.

recipes.title: Paleo Spiced Roasted Butternut Squash Recipe
ingredients: 1 whole butternut squash, stems removed, peeled, and seeded
instructions: Preheat oven to 450F. Cut both ends, peel the butternut squash, and seed it. Discard skin and seeds. Cut the squash into small evenly-sized rectangles and transfer to a sheet tray lined with foil.
Mix the spice mix, including the salt, in a small bowl. Season the butternut squash liberally with the spice mix and add the oil. Mix with hands to fully incorporate.
Roast in the preheated oven for 35 minutes. Pierce with a fork to test doneness. There should be no resistance. If there is, add 10 minutes and continue roasting.

Remove from oven, cool down and store in an airtight container, or serve 3 oz per dish for exact macros. Conversely, if you are serving this for dinner, serve immediately with your preferred protein and paleo vegetables.
recipes.summary: This paleo recipe provides a great mix of spices that compliment each other, and combine incredibly well with the roasted butternut squash's natural flavor.

recipes.title: Paleo Creamy Bacon & Spaghetti Squash Recipe
ingredients: 1 whole spaghetti squash, ends cut, halved lengthwise
3 bacon strips, baked crispy, roughly chopped
Kosher salt and freshly ground pepper, to taste
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 F. Remove both ends of the spaghetti squash and then cut it in half lengthwise. Scoop out any seeds, and discard. Season with salt, freshly ground pepper, and the 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Massage into the spaghetti squash to season evenly. Get two sheet trays lined with foil. In one of them, lay the two halves of the spaghetti squash face down so that the skin of the squash is facing up.

In the second sheet tray, lay the 3 bacon strips next to each other without overlapping them. Place both sheet trays in the preheated oven. Set a 12-minute timer for the bacon and a 45-minute timer for the spaghetti squash. After the 12 minutes have passed, remove the bacon from the oven, and let cool on paper towels. Once cool, rough chop and transfer to a small bowl. Keep aside.

Prepare the garlic, coconut milk, maple syrup, and apple cider vinegar (pre-measure them in small individual containers or have them on the side for easy addition. Remove the spaghetti squash from the oven and poke with a fork. If the fork pierces through without any resistance, the squash is done cooking. Otherwise, add 10 minutes in the oven and cook until the fork pierces the squash without any resistance. Let cool on the side or for a faster process, transfer to a room temp tray, and cool down in the fridge.

Once cold to the touch, scrape the spaghetti squash off the skin using a fork. Turn the squash upside down, so that the skin is facing down and scrape from one end to another. Transfer contents to a bowl. Heat 1 tablespoon of avocado oil on a medium skillet, over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for 2-3 minutes stirring often. This will slowly caramelize the garlic and will also infuse the oil with a garlic flavor, thus constructing an aromatic base flavor layer to the dish. Add the bacon and cook for another 1 to 2 minutes. Deglaze the pan with apple cider vinegar. Mix, and add the coconut milk. Mix and add the spaghetti squash. Mix and add maple syrup. Season with kosher salt and black pepper. Bring to a boil and slightly reduce the liquid. Remove from heat and cool down. Store in containers or serve immediately.
recipes.summary: This delicious paleo recipe mixes the sweetness of the spaghetti squash with savory notes of the bacon and a healthy dose of sweetness and fattiness.

recipes.title: Roasted Paleo Cauliflower with Agrodolce Sauce Recipe
instructions: Preheat oven to 425 F. Cut the cauliflower into florets and transfer it to a foil-lined baking sheet pan. Season with kosher salt and 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Roast for 15 minutes. Set a timer.

Meanwhile, combine sliced rounds of Fresno chiles, red wine vinegar, olive oil, honey, red pepper flakes, and raisins in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil and then reduce heat to a slow simmer to allow the sauce to thicken up for 20 minutes. Remove from heat once thickened.

Remove cauliflower from the oven once done roasting and transfer into a bowl. Pour agrodolce sauce over the cauliflower and toss to coat. Serve immediately or cool down completely if using for meal prep.
recipes.summary: Our agrodolce sauce recipe with red wine vinegar, honey, raisins, along with a kick of spice from the peppers compliment the roasted cauliflower flavor perfectly.

recipes.title: Paleo Aromatic Greens with Coconut Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups kale, destemmed, leaves washed and roughly chopped
2 cups collard greens, destemmed, leaves washed and rough chopped
4 scallions, bottoms removed, white bulb cut into round thin circles
instructions: Prepare your leafy greens by washing them, destemming and chopping into 3” wide cuts. You can de-stem the kale by simply scraping your hands from the bottom to the top of the stem and removing the kale leaves in the process.For the collard greens, you’ll have to cut the stem off with the tip of your knife by cutting the leaves off alongside both edges of the stem. Measure four cups of each and keep aside in a bowl. Discard stems.

Prepare your aromatics as indicated. Mince your garlic, shallots, and Microplane the ginger all into one small bowl. In a separate bowl, cut the white part of the scallions. Discard the greens or store for later use in another dish or as a garnish. Heat 1 tablespoons of coconut oil in a deep large round pan over medium heat. Once the oil is shimmering, add your aromatics all at once and stir often. Cook for 1-2 minutes or until aromatics are fragrant. Add scallions. Cook stirring constantly for another 1-2 minutes. Season with salt.

Immediately add your leafy greens, a bunch at a time, allowing each batch to slightly wilt and cook to create more room in the pan. Scrape any browned bits. Once all 4 cups of greens are in the pan, deglaze the pan with the coconut milk and mix. Scrape any browned bits. Bring to a boil, and then remove from heat. Serve immediately or cool down quickly if meal prepping. Don’t forget to add the lime juice at the end if serving immediately or serve with a lime wedge if meal prepping.
recipes.summary: The bitterness of the hearty greens, built on an aromatic base, mixed with the fattiness and sweetness of the coconut and maple syrup makes a great side dish.

recipes.title: Vegan Tofu Tikka Masala Recipe
ingredients: 16 ounces of store-bought baked tofu, or 1
Trifecta A la Carte white rice
instructions: Preheat the oven to 450 degrees fahrenheit. Trim the cauliflower into small florets and, in a bowl, mix together the cauliflower, coconut oil, nutritional yeast, garam masala, and lime juice. Spread out the cauliflower onto a baking tray lined with a reusable liner or parchment paper. Bake for 20 minutes, until slightly golden, then remove from the oven. Cut the baked tofu into medium-sized pieces or prepare one recipe fried tofu and set aside.
Prepare the tikka masala sauce
Add your roasted vegetables and tofu to the simmering tikka masala sauce and cook for 5 minutes, then remove from the heat.
Serve 1-1.5 cups of masala with 1/2 cup of rice and top with chopped cilantro.
recipes.summary: This creamy and cozy vegan tikka masala recipe will have you inspired to spice up all your go-to recipes.

recipes.title: Creamy Vegan Tikka Masala Sauce Recipe
instructions: First mince the ginger, garlic, and onion in a food processor to create a chunky paste. This will bring out all of the ingredients aromatic oils and enhance the flavor of the sauce. If you don’t have a food processor, sharpen up your knife skills and mince everything by hand with a chef’s knife; make sure to get the pieces as small as possible.

Heat a medium-sized pot or saucepan over medium-high heat and add 1 tablespoon of coconut oil then place in the minced aromatics. Stirring occasionally, cook the aromatics for 10 minutes until they begin to caramelize and brown.

Continuing to cook on medium-high heat, add your ground spices and cook for 1 minute until the spices are toasted slightly and you can smell a warm and rich bouquet of spices in the air. Stir in the diced tomatoes, and cook for 2 more minutes until the mixture begins to thicken. Add the stock and coconut milk and stir to release any more of the fond on the bottom of the pot then simmer for 5 minutes on medium-high heat.

Finish the sauce by mixing in the vegan yogurt and lemon juice then simmer for 2-3 more minutes until it has thickened slightly. Season with additional lemon juice, pinch of salt, and even a pinch of coconut sugar for a well-rounded and flavorful vegan tikka masala sauce.
recipes.summary: This creamy and cozy dairy-free vegan tikka masala recipe will have you inspired to spice up all your go-to recipes.

recipes.title: Paleo Cashew Chicken Recipe
ingredients: Trifecta A La Carte chicken breast
4 scallions, washed, rough chopped, white and green bulbs separated
instructions: If you are using Trifecta A La Carte chicken, remove the chicken from the container and dice it. Keep in a medium bowl on the side until needed. If using raw chicken, dice the chicken, transfer to a baking tray, and season with salt. For either method, sprinkle the 2 Tbsp. of the arrowroot powder along with a splash of water and spread to coat evenly. Set aside. Wash your station and cutting board, washing all surfaces where the raw chicken came in contact.

Cut both bell peppers into large square pieces, and keep them separated in a bowl. Next, slice the white bulbs of the scallions, separating them from the green bulbs. Keep in separate bowls. Make the sauce by combining all the ingredients listed above. Bring to a boil and then reduce the heat to simmer. Simmer over low heat until needed.

Heat up 1 tablespoon coconut oil in a large round skillet, or a wok if you have it. Once coconut oil has melted and is shimmering, add the bell peppers plus the scallions and cook over high heat for a minute or two. Season with a sprinkle of salt. Immediately add the chicken, cooking it for about 3-5 minutes over medium heat, adjusting it as needed, and making sure all sides of the chicken get as crisp and brown as possible. Add the sauce and purposefully scrape any browned bits left on the bottom of the pan. Simmer the sauce for another 2 minutes, or until it thickens up.
Reduce the heat if needed. Add the cashews to the pan, and mix to fully incorporate. Remove from the heat and portion 4.5 ounces of the chicken per container for exact macros.
recipes.summary: This delicious Asian-inspired paleo cashew chicken recipe is both sweet and salty, complete with the perfect amount of crunch and nuttiness from the cashews.

recipes.title: Paleo Mongolian Beef Recipe
ingredients: 4 each scallion, sliced into small rounds
4 Fresno peppers, or if unavailable, 1 red bell pepper, diced small
instructions: Whether using raw steak or Trifecta’s A La Carte steak, using a sharp knife, thinly slice the steak lengthwise as evenly as possible. If needed, half the steak across and then slice lengthwise. Transfer to a bowl and keep to the side.

In a separate bowl, combine the coconut aminos, red boat fish sauce, arrowroot flour, baking soda, sesame oil, and maple syrup. Mix to incorporate and pour over sliced steak. Refrigerate for 10 minutes.

Prepare all your aromatics by cutting the white bulbs of the scallions and keeping the green stalks separated, grating the ginger, slicing the garlic, and combining them all in a small bowl. Cut the Fresno peppers or red bell peppers into small cubes. Remove the seeds completely and discard. If using raw beef, heat 1 tablespoon of avocado oil over medium-high heat and sear both sides for 3-5 minutes. Remove from pan and reserve the now cooked beef on the side. Reserve the marinade. (If using Trifecta A La Carte steak ignore this step)

Heat remaining 1 tablespoon of avocado oil in the same pan over medium heat (if using raw steak). Once oil is warm and shimmering, add your reserved aromatics and cook, stirring constantly to avoid over charring. Add the Fresno peppers and season with a pinch of salt.

Add the beef (if you are using Trifecta’s A La carte, simply add the steak now), along with half of the marinade. This will deglaze the pan. Stir and scrape any browned bits. Cook for a minimum of 5 minutes or until the sauce has thickened and evenly coated the steak, adding a beautiful glaze. Portion the Mongolian beef in 4 ounce servings for exact macros. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced green scallions.
recipes.summary: Mongolian beef has a unique sweet and savory flavor base that is scallion and ginger forward, with a slight spice. Find a fun paleo recipe to use any day.

recipes.title: Paleo Thai Turkey Larb Recipe
ingredients: 3-4 dry red Thai chiles, toasted, destemmed, seeded, ground, or 1 ½ Tbsp. red Thai chile powder
½ cup thinly sliced shallots (fine julienne)
⅓ cup fresh picked mint, washed
4-8 butter lettuce wraps, washed, shocked in iced water
instructions: If using Trifecta’s Meal Prep turkey patties, crumble them using a food processor for a fast and efficient process (we recommend Breville's food processor). Break the patties into smaller pieces and then add to the food processor. Pulse until small crumbles form.

If using whole dry red Thai chiles, destem and remove as many seeds as possible and toast the peppers on a dry skillet over medium heat until fragrant and somewhat sweet. Transfer to a spice grinder, and grind until a powder is formed. Be super careful when opening the lid of the grinder, as this might cause excessive sneezing and coughing. I recommend you wear a mask as a further precaution.

Add ground turkey in a wide skillet, along with a ⅓ cup water. Bring to a quick boil, reduce heat to low, and right before the water is fully evaporated, add 2 tablespoons of fish sauce, mix to fully incorporate, and then add the honey. Mix again. Remove from heat and transfer to a sheet pan. Cool in the freezer to bring down the temperature quickly.

Prepare the shallots by cutting them into thin long strips, and reserving them in a bowl on the side. Wash and pick your mint, and reserve on the side. Rough chop the cilantro and also keep in a side bowl. Pick 4-8 Boston bib lettuce leaves and wash. Keep to the side. Use only 4 lettuce leaves if you’d like to portion one leaf per serving, or prepare 8 if you’d prefer two per serving

Once the ground turkey is fully cooled, transfer to a medium bowl and add the ingredients in this order, always mixing before adding the following ingredient to fully incorporate. Start by adding 1 ½ tablespoon of Thai chili powder, followed by shallots, fresh-picked mint, chopped cilantro, and finish with lime juice. Taste for seasoning and add a pinch of salt if needed. The fish sauce is salty enough that this step may not be necessary. Serve 4.5 ounces of the Larb on 1 or 2 nestled Boston bib lettuce wraps.
recipes.summary: Find a very specific step by step process on how to properly build a Thai larb dish using chile powder, lime juice, shallots, fresh herbs and fish sauce.

recipes.title: Paleo Gluten-Free Bread Recipe
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350F. In a bowl, mix all dry ingredients together: the almond flour, tapioca flour, rice flour and baking soda.

In a smaller bowl whisk the egg whites until you get soft peaks. Feel free to use an electric mixer for a faster stiffing of the egg whites. In a separate bowl, crack the whole eggs and whisk. Add the vinegar, avocado oil, honey, and almond milk. Whisk once again to mix. Fold the egg whites into the whole eggs mix by using a spatula. First, add a small amount of soft peak egg whites into the whole eggs, and gently fold them into the whole egg mixture with the spatula (folding means to combine two liquids of different volumes into one uniform mixture). Once the initial egg white mix is folded in, repeat the same process folding in the rest of the egg whites in small quantities, until the whites and whole eggs are one uniform liquid mix.

Immediately mix the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients by adding the dry ingredients a third at a time. Similar to the egg white folding, carefully and slowly blend the dry ingredients in (do so in small batches at a time). Repeat until all ingredients are mixed in. Do not overmix.

Liberally grease a loaf pan with oil. Add the mixture to the pan, spread evenly, flatten, and bake for 45 minutes in a convection oven. It may take about 1 hour in a non-conventional (no fan) oven. After 45 minutes, remove loaf from the oven and let rest for 15 minutes to up to an hour. Remove from the pan and slice into 1 oz thick slices.
recipes.summary: No gluten? No problem. Try our incredibly well-designed paleo gluten-free bread recipe that resembles a kind of banana-bread and satisfies any bread craving.

recipes.title: Vegan Southwest Baked Sweet Potato Recipe
ingredients: Trifecta A la Carte Beyond Meat® burgers,
crumbled or 1 (16 oz) package Beyond Meat burgers®
¼ cup vegetable stock or water
1 Roma tomato, diced or ½ cup pico de gallo
instructions: To bake your potato in less time, cut your sweet potatoes in half lengthwise and rub with oil (utilize coconut oil for extra flavor), place sweet potatoes cut side down on a baking sheet lined with foil and roast for 25-30 minutes until tender. Move onto the next steps while your sweet potatoes are baking in the oven.
In a food processor or blender, add the cilantro, vegan yogurt, lime juice, and chili powder. Blend until smooth, then place crema into a separate container and store in the fridge until needed.

To prepare your filling, heat a large saute pan over medium-high heat and add 1 teaspoon oil, then add the garlic and onion. Saute the garlic and onion for 4 minutes until they are fragrant and begin to brown.
Crumble the Beyond Meat or Trifecta A la Carte Beyond Meat burger patties into the saute pan and cook for 5 minutes, mixing with the onion and garlic

Add the corn, bell pepper, and spices, continuing to cook over medium-high heat for 2 more minutes then finish by adding lime juice and stock or water. Cook for an additional minute then remove from heat.
Build your sweet potato by layering the filling into the baked potato, then topping with sliced green onion, avocado, and the cilantro crema.
recipes.summary: Our vegan Southwest baked sweet potato recipe only requires a few fresh ingredients, spices, and a baked sweet potato for a satisfyingly simple meal.

recipes.title: Paleo Chicken Broccoli Recipe
ingredients: 16 oz chicken raw, diced and roasted, or 16 oz of Trifecta chicken, diced
instructions: Preheat oven to 400F. Dice chicken into even cubes and transfer the chicken to a medium bowl. Season lightly with salt and pepper and keep to the side. Clean your station by washing your knife and cutting board.

If using raw chicken, roast your chicken in the oven for 12 minutes. Once ready, remove from the oven and cool down. If using Trifecta A La Carte Chicken, cube the chicken and transfer to a medium bowl until needed. Clean your station. Cut the broccoli into even florets. Season with salt, pepper, and avocado oil. Roast for 12 minutes in the oven. Feel free to do this while the chicken is cooking.
If using Trifecta’s A La Carte Broccoli, remove all 16 ounces from the container and lightly season with salt and pepper. Hold on the side as there is no need to roast.

In a small saucepan, combine sesame oil, avocado oil, chicken broth, coconut aminos, lime juice, fresh ginger, honey garlic powder, and red pepper flakes. Bring to a boil, mix often, and then reduce heat to medium to an active simmer.

Prepare your cornstarch slurry. The slurry will thicken the sauce - we don’t want to reduce the sauce too much as the salt will get stronger as the liquid evaporates. Add 1 teaspoon at a time, mixing constantly. 2 teaspoons of the slurry should suffice but feel free to add extra for further sauce body.

Once the sauce has thickened, pour on top of the cooked cubed chicken. Plate with 1 cup of roasted broccoli on each plate, and 4 ounces of chicken right on top. Sprinkle with any remaining additional sauce. Garnish with sesame seeds.
recipes.summary: A super simple paleo chicken recipe made for a quick dinner night or to satisfy your meal prep efforts.

recipes.title: Gluten-Free Paleo Meatloaf Recipe
ingredients: ½ cup parsley, fresh, minced, garnish
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375F. Mince the shallots and garlic and combine them in a small bowl. Cook in a saute pan with 1 tablespoon avocado oil until translucent. Season lightly and cool.

In a separate medium bowl, combine the ground beef, almond flour, ground flaxseed, honey, red pepper flakes, 1 tablespoon of coconut amino, kosher salt, black pepper, and the cooled shallot-garlic mix. Mix with your hands to fully combine all ingredients, but avoid overworking your ground beef.

Once ingredients are fully incorporated, spray your baking pan with spray oil liberally. Transfer the beef mixture to the pan and press down until fully flat. Transfer pan to oven and bake for 1 hour at the preset temperature. Bake until internal temperature reaches 160F or the full 45 minutes. Whichever happens first. Remove from oven and let carryover cooking cook to 165F.

Meanwhile, as the meatloaf cooks, in a separate small bowl mix all your ketchup ingredients. Taste for seasoning and adjust as needed. As well, mince and mix your green herb garnish. Keep aside covered until needed.

Once the meatloaf is done, remove from the oven. Spread the ketchup and brush top of it until the spread is spread evenly. Broil for 3-5 minutes. Let the meatloaf rest for 10 minutes before cutting. Using a sharp serrated knife, slice the loaf about 1” thick.
If meal prepping, portion to 5 oz portions sizes for exact macros below, cool, and then store in the fridge, or serve immediately.
recipes.summary: Find a simple, paleo-friendly meatloaf recipe, with an even easier tasty home-made ketchup that can be made ahead of time.

recipes.title: Paleo Chocolate Protein Pudding Recipe
ingredients: 14 fl oz. coconut milk, unsweetened, canned, 2 fl oz separated
instructions: In a small or medium saucepan, combine 12 fl oz of the coconut milk, dutch cocoa powder, maple syrup, and vanilla extract. Bring to a boil slowly, stirring occasionally.

In a separate bowl, combine the arrowroot powder and the protein powder with the reserved 2 fl oz coconut milk. Whisk to fully incorporate. Slowly add the coconut-arrowroot mix to the pan as you whisk it in. Bring to a boil and reduce the heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Take the pan off the heat and let cool to room temperature for 15 minutes.
You can weigh and create your own puddings as part of your meal prep or simply transfer the pudding to a meal prep container and refrigerate covered. Serve into 4.8 oz portions.
recipes.summary: This straight-forward paleo chocolate protein pudding recipe will satisfy any sweet cravings you may have while providing a beautifully balanced nutrition profile

recipes.title: Paleo Chicken Pesto Stuffed Sweet Potato Recipe
ingredients: Salt and black pepper, to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 425F. Cut potatoes lengthwise in half. Season the now four halves with salt and pepper, nutritional yeast, and avocado oil. Place seasoned potatoes on a foil-lined baking sheet, and bake potatoes facing down in the preheated oven for 45 minutes.
Meanwhile, make the pesto sauce as indicated by the recipe. Keep aside.
Use Trifecta pulled chicken breast and dress it with pesto sauce in a medium bowl.

Once potatoes are baked, remove from the oven, and carefully flip them around, keeping the skin intact. Make a slight indent with the back of a spoon and plate 4 ounces of the dressed pulled pesto chicken. Serve with a lemon wedge on the side and with your favorite vegetables.
recipes.summary: Highly delicious shredded pesto chicken sitting on a half-baked sweet potato, perfect recipe for your paleo meal prep.

recipes.title: 15 High Protein Vegan Recipes to Make This Week
instructions: 1. Be Precise
2. Use A Variety of Plant-Based Proteins
3. Diversify Your Carbohydrates
4. Eat More Non-Starchy Vegetables
5. Add High Protein Snacks
6. Top off Your Meal Prep with Healthy Garnishes
7. Get A Quality Protein Powder
5. Vegan Beyond Burger Chili Recipe
recipes.summary: High protein vegan recipes designed to help you sustain and enjoy your plant-based diet!

recipes.title: The Only Vegan Taco Recipe You'll Ever Need
ingredients: 8 taco shells, hard or soft (we used corn-based shells)
½ cup pico de gallo, store bought or homemade
instructions: Blend your taco seasoning by whisking together the spices until evenly combined.

To prepare your taco filling first place your skillet over medium-high heat. Add in the oil, and saute the minced onion and garlic for 3 minutes until they begin to brown. Add your beyond meat and cook for 4 minutes then mix in 2-3 tablespoons taco spice and continue to cook until meat is golden brown.
Add 1/2 cup vegetable stock and saute meat and spices for 1-2 minutes until the stock has been reduced by half.
To prepare your taco toppings, rinse the cilantro and roughly chop then set aside in a small bowl. Slice the lime into wedges and set aside as well.
Now you're ready to plate your tacos or package it up to enjoy later on!
recipes.summary: Here is the secret to creating this rich and glorious meaty vegan taco recipe; Beyond Meat® and a bomb taco seasoning mix.

recipes.title: Vegan California Burger Recipe
ingredients: 4 burger buns (sesame, potato, or gluten-free; note the difference in nutrition, we used classic potato buns)
1 cup fresh Sprouts (alfalfa, broccoli, mung bean, radish, or sunflower)
1 head bib lettuce, cleaned and split into individual leaves
¼ cup mint or parsley chopped
instructions: Prepare the guacamole by removing the seed from the avocado and mashing together with minced garlic, lime, a pinch of salt, and paprika.
Prepare the kiwi pomegranate salsa by small dicing the kiwi, then mixing with the pomegranate seeds, cilantro, and minced ginger.
Set aside all of your burger toppings: sprouts, lettuce, sliced tomato, guacamole, and salsa.
Toast your burger buns in the oven or toaster oven.
Grill the Beyond Meat burgers for 3 minutes on each side; if using Trifecta a la carte Beyond burgers, heat for 1-2 minutes on each side.
Build your burger and dig in!
recipes.summary: With a tangy kiwi pomegranate salsa and garlicky guacamole, this juicy vegan California burger recipe is packed with 30 grams of plant-based protein.

recipes.title: Almond and Walnut Tabbouleh Recipe
ingredients: ¼ cup olive oil or avocado oil
¼ cup walnuts, toasted and crushed
¼ cup almonds, toasted and crushed
instructions: Toast your almonds and walnuts for 6-8 minutes in a 375-degree oven until slightly golden and fragrant.
Remove nuts from the ovens, let cool for 5 minutes then crush and place in a large bowl.
Rinse and dry your herbs then mince the parsley with the stems on and add to the large bowl. Remove the basil and mint leaves from their stems and mince the leaves then add to the parsley.
Using a microplane or fine-grater, grate the peeled ginger and garlic clove into the large bowl.
Measure out the rest of the ingredients and toss together in the bowl, season additionally if desired
recipes.summary: This refreshing almond and walnut tabbouleh recipe is a vegan, keto, paleo, and clean-eating friendly twist prepared with nutmeg and sumac.

recipes.title: Vegan Tofu Tabbouleh Bowl Recipe
ingredients: 3-4 small beets, roasted at home, or store-bought sliced
or 1 package of firm tofu cut into cubes
instructions: Prepare your fried tofu, tabbouleh, and quinoa according to recipe directions. Combine the quinoa and 2 cups tabbouleh together to marinade. You should have extra tabbouleh for finishing your bowl.

Wash your vegetables and greens. Remove the stems from the kale and finely chop. Using a vegetable peeler, peel the carrot into fine strips. Toss the kale, carrots, and chickpeas together and set aside.
Remove the peel from your orange and slice into slices. Do this over a bowl to save any orange juice and mix it into the tabbouleh.
To build your bowl: measure 1/3 cup tabbouleh and quinoa mixture, 4 ounces tofu, 1/2 cup roasted beets, 3-4 sliced orange, 1 cup kale, carrot, and chickpea salad. Top with extra tabbouleh if desired.
recipes.summary: Dig into this phytonutrient dense vegan tofu tabbouleh bowl recipe is composed of fried tofu, quinoa, and fresh vegetables tossed with a refreshing almond and walnut tabbouleh

recipes.title: Meal Prep Roasted Beets like a Boss
ingredients: At least a cup of apple cider vinegar
instructions: Preheat your oven to 425 degrees
Trim the tops of the beets, separate them by color and size if needed. Place like colors into a baking dish.
Add enough apple cider vinegar and water to cover the beets almost halfway with liquid. Cover the dish with foil then place in the oven.
Bake for 35 to 45 minutes or more depending on the size of the beets. Test by taking a fork or small toothpick and gently piercing the beet.
Remove from the oven carefully and let cool for 10 minutes.
Peel beets using disposable paper towels or a vegetable peeler.
Cut as desired, and store for future use in your weekly meal prep or tasty recipe!
recipes.summary: Skip the boiling and go straight to the oven for naturally sweet roasted beets.

recipes.title: Keto Salmon on Lemon-Parm Kale Salad Recipe
ingredients: 4 - 4 oz Salmon fillets, baked to 145F or
1 tablespoon fresh or dried rosemary, chopped (optional)
4 cups kale, stem removed, thinly sliced
Kosher salt and black pepper to taste
4 tablespoons seeds or nuts: pistachios, chia seeds, sunflower seeds, and pumpkin seeds (optional)
Step One: Prepare Your Kale Salad
Gather all of your ingredients and first prepare your kale salad.

Remove the kale from the tough stems and discard the stems as the stems tend to be fibrous and tough. Chop up the kale leaves and place them into a bowl. Squeeze over the lemon juice, and then using your hands or a pair of tongs massage in the olive oil and lemon juice into your kale. This is a super simple vinaigrette you can make ahead of time and keep in your fridge in a jar.
Marinade your kale a day ahead if you'd like, since kale is a hearty green it will stay fresh and crisp even after being tossed with a dressing!
At this point, you can also add in your parmesan cheese, and any additional nuts, seeds, and mix-ins you would like!
Using a variety of nuts and seeds will add extra healthy fat for your meal but also layer in additional trace vitamins and minerals.
, remove 4 filets from the Trifecta package and gently separate them. There are several ways to re-heat Trifecta Meal Prep Salmon:

: To reheat your Trifecta salmon in the oven, preheat your oven to 400 degrees and place salmon on a baking sheet lined with foil. Once the oven is warmed bake for 8 minutes or until warmed through. Top with fresh lemon slices and herbs before baking to add extra flavor!
: This is the quickest way to reheat your salmon, simply place it on a microwave-safe dish and heat for 2-3 minutes until warmed through.
: A great option if you enjoy a slightly crispy exterior to your salmon. To
, preheat your air-fried to 400 degrees and air-fry the salmon for 4-7 minutes, depending on how crispy you'd like your salmon to be.

: We choose to reheat the salmon for this recipe via stovetop. Simple heat a non-stick pan over medium-high heat and add 1 teaspoon of avocado oil, gently place in salmon filets, and cook for 2 minutes on each side until golden brown; add in some fresh lemon and herbs during the process to add extra flavor.
If you are preparing this for your weekly meal prep, you don't need to cook your trifecta salmon, simple heat a portion of the salmon when you are ready to enjoy your salad!
If you are cooking the salmon from raw, preheat the oven to 400F, line a baking sheet with foil, spray it with spray oil, and season both sides of the salmon with salt and pepper.

Season with salt and pepper if you haven't done so and bake for 12-15 minutes, or until the internal temperature marks 145F. This is typically when the salmon turns opaque and you can see the white fat start to emerge on the surface and sides of the filet.
Remove your salmon from the oven, and transfer to a dish to cool in the fridge before portioning onto your salads or serve if eating, serve immediately.
Step Three: Season, Portion, and Voila!
If you are preparing this for your weekly meal prep, divide your salad into four even containers and top with your salmon and extra parmesan cheese!

As mentioned above, you can dress the kale and store it in the fridge for up to five days. Check on the quality of the kale and salmon before eating, if you keep these in the fridge for longer than 5 days.
If you’re adding this great recipe to your weekly keto meal prep, remember you can cross-utilize the ingredients! Use this kale salad and pair it with

Squeeze over the lemon juice, and then using your hands or a pair of tongs massage in the olive oil and lemon juice into your kale. recipes.title: How To Make Scrambled Eggs For Meal Prep: 5 Professional Chef Tips
ingredients: Whole eggs, quantity based on weekly needs
instructions: Find a container or a bowl deep enough to hold all your eggs at once. Place this container in your sink (ideally) and set a fine-mesh strainer on top of the container. Crack the eggs into the strainer. Cracking the eggs in the strainer will help you avoid getting eggshells into your mix and makes for easier cleanup. Conversely, you can simply crack your eggs into the bowl, and then strain them into a separate container to get rid of any small eggshell debris that may have gotten into the egg mix.

Once you are ready to cook, figure out the number of eggs you need to weigh and cook for the following few days. For example: if you are cooking 3 days worth of eggs, and you eat 4 eggs per breakfast (3 days x 213g (or 4 eggs) raw eggs) that would be an equivalent of 639g of raw eggs to be cooked at once for your meal prep. Keep your total measured egg quantity in a container.

Heat your pan over medium-high heat and spray liberally with spray oil. As soon as the oil is warm (shimmering or just slightly smoking), immediately add your eggs into the pan. Do not excessively fill the pan with raw eggs. Overfilling will make cooking the eggs hard and more likely than not will cause you to spill eggs onto the stovetop as you are scrambling them. Season your eggs with salt and pepper to taste, and begin scrambling by gently and constantly moving the eggs in the pan. Reduce the heat to medium if the eggs As the eggs scramble, continue to move the spatula around and break any large chunks of cooked eggs. Once at 90% cooked, remove the eggs from the heat and gently scoop them into a sheet tray. Spread evenly. Repeat the process if you have any eggs left to cook. Cooldown completely in the fridge uncovered cooking too fast.

Once eggs are cooled down, and measure according to the quantity of eggs you eat per day. Use the chart above as guide to know exactly how much you will need to portion on each container to hit your macros!
recipes.summary: Find out how to make scrambled eggs for meal prep with these 5 life-chanign tips as suggested by Trifecta's Executive Chef!

recipes.title: 6 Vegan Breakfast Recipes For Plant-Based Power
instructions: 1. Vegan Mediterranean Beyond Breakfast Skillet

recipes.title: Protein Overnight Oats Recipe
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl and mix.
Transfer the ingredients to a jar, cover with lid or plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight.
In the morning, remove lid/plastic wrap and add toppings as desired. Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Meal prep this protein overnight oats recipe in less than 10 minutes for a macro balanced, healthy breakfast all week long!

recipes.title: Keto Guacatillo Chicken Salad Wrap Recipe
ingredients: 1 Lb or 16 ounces chicken, cooked, diced, or use
4 teaspoons slivered almonds nuts, lightly roasted
8 Romaine lettuce leaves, shocked and rinsed
2 Baking sheet, 1 large & 1 small with foil
Step One: Dice and Cook the Chicken
Dice the raw chicken and combine into a medium bowl with salt and pepper, mix to evenly season the chicken. Spread chicken evenly on a foil-lined baking sheet sprayed with oil.
, skip this step, and simply dice the chicken breast!
To avoid cross-contamination and potential food-borne illness, wash your cutting board, knife, hands, and any other surface where the raw chicken breast was handled.
Bake for 14 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through (165F internal temp). Remove from the oven and cool at room temperature for 5 min. then transfer to a fridge to finish cooling down.

as directed on the recipe. Feel free to make this recipe ahead of time and have it available for your meal prep rotation. The sauce will naturally oxidize over time and begin turning brown (due to the avocado); so be sure to use the sauce within 5 days of making it.
To prevent avocado based sauces from oxidizing, turning brown, add ascorbic, citric acid, extra lemon juice to curve the oxidization process.
In the oven, lightly toast the pistachios and slivered almonds for 5 minutes then remove and let cool at room temp.
Prepare your lettuce wraps by cutting off the bottom core portion of the lettuce. Separate the largest nicest looking leaves, for a total of 8 whole lettuce leaves and prepare to shock them.
Fill a large bowl with ice water (half ice + half water), and carefully 'shock' the lettuce leaves for ~5 minutes. Halve cherry tomatoes and keep them aside in a small container until needed.
"Shocking" is a culinary term that means to plunge greens in ice water to keep the color bright and the texture crip (crissssspppp - that's right).
Step Three: Put It all Together

Once all ingredients are cooled and ready, use a scale to measure 4 ounces of the diced chicken, 2 tablespoon of the Guacatillo sauce, 1 tablespoon of pistachios, 1 teaspoon of slivered almonds, a few halved cherry tomatoes, and a dash of Tabasco. Mix gently to fully incorporate the sauce into all ingredients.
Divide the chicken mix into two Romaine lettuce leaves. Sprinkle a small amount of paprika for color and garnish, plus 1 teaspoon of cotija cheese. Store in an airtight container.
The macros for this recipe are:
330 kcal; 21g Fat; 5g Net Carb (11g CHO - 6g DF); 28g Protein. A perfectly balanced meal.
Helpful Tips for Storing and Serving

If you are preparing multiple of these for the week, store the chicken mixed with the rest of the ingredients in one container, and the rinsed Romaine lettuce leaves in a separate container. Plate the night prior. This will help retain the freshness of the lettuce as the week goes by.
Also feel free to use the chicken mixture for any other kind of meal prep dishes. Have fun and get creative with this recipe!

recipes.title: Guacatillo Sauce Recipe
ingredients: 4 Avocados pitted and removed from the skin
instructions: Prepare all ingredients as indicated and combine them in a bowl. Reserve the lemon juice on the side.
Transfer all ingredients to a blender and using a tamper, blend starting at a slow speed. Slowly increase the speed as the sauce becomes smooth and velvety. Blend for 1-2 minutes.
Add the lime juice towards the end of the blending time. Taste for seasoning and adjust as needed.
Transfer to an air-tight container and store in the fridge for 5 days for maximum freshness.
recipes.summary: Mouth coating, slightly tangy and sweet, and rich in body, this Guacatillo sauce recipe goes on everything from proteins, to salads, and even vegetables!

recipes.title: Crispy Thai Steak Salad Recipe
ingredients: 1 Thai chili pepper, minced or serrano pepper
2 cups Zucchini Noodles or Shaved Zucchini
1/4 cup cashews or almonds, roasted and chopped
instructions: Mix all ingredients for the marinade together in a small bowl then toss with cubbed steak and set aside for 4-5 minutes.
To prepare Thai Peanut dressing, mix all ingredients together in a medium bowl, and adjust with water if too thick.
Toss zucchini, slaw mix, and salad dressing together.
To cook steak, heat a skillet over medium-high heat, add oil and saute for 2-3 minutes until lightly browned and heated through. Remove from pan and plate with salad.
Top salad with toasted cashews and additional fresh herbs.
recipes.summary: A Thai steak salad recipe with a Thai-inspired peanut dressing and sesame marinated steak tossed with crunchy cruciferous vegetables, and refreshing herbs.

recipes.title: Baked Salmon Pasta Piccata Recipe
ingredients: 4 servings pasta of your choice, cooked
¼ cup mozzarella or parmesan cheese
1/3 cup vegetables stock or chicken stock
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook your pasta of choice according to packaged instructions.
Remove the salmon from packaging into a bowl and shred into large chunks. Do the same with the broccoli.
To prepare the piccata sauce heat a sauté pan over medium heat, add butter, onions, and garlic. Sweat for 2 minutes then add fennel and rosemary; cook for 2 more minutes.
Add lemon juice, parsley, capers, cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the broth or water then bring to a soft simmer and cook until emulsified about 2-3 minutes.
Remove from heat and taste, adjust if needed.
Combine with broccoli, salmon, and pasta in a baking dish and top with cheese. Bake for 5 minutes until cheese has melted and salmon is warmed through.
recipes.summary: Salmon smothered in tangy lemon-butter, piccata sauce tossed with pasta and broccoli, all baked into one tasty and quick recipe.

recipes.title: Low-Carb Carne Asada Steak Taco Recipe
ingredients: Trifecta Meal Prep Flat Iron Steak
¼ cup cilantro with the stems, roughly chopped
2 teaspoon chili powder or smoked paprika
of your choice or pico de gallo
Medium grill pan, saute pan or Air-Fryer
instructions: Combine all the marinade ingredients into a bowl and then place into a zip-lock baggie or container with the steak. Let marinade for 5 minutes at the least before cooking, for the best results marinade overnight or up to 2 days.

On the Grill or Grill Pan: Heat your grill to medium-high heat, brush the grates or grill pan with oil, and grill your Trifecta A La Carte steak for 1-2 minutes per side until charred and heated through.
Refer to alternative cooking methods above, saute or air-fryer if desired.
Remove your steak from the heat and let it rest for 1-2 minutes before slicing into it to preserve the juices. Once sliced, toss with extra marinade for a flavor boost.
Once you have prepared your steak, pick your toppings, and then fill your shell! I went with a simple homemade pico, fresh cilantro, and onion.
recipes.summary: Love tacos but need to watch your carb intake? Enjoy this quick low-carb carne asada steak taco recipe prepared with a crunchy low carb jicama shell.

recipes.title: Unbelievable Italian Caprese Salmon Recipe
ingredients: 4 (4 oz) Trifecta Meal Prep Salmon fillets
instructions: Pre-heat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat a saute pan over medium-high heat, add 1 teaspoon oil and heat for 30-45 seconds.

Saute the grape tomatoes for 1-2 minutes until blistered, add the spinach, and ½ of the balsamic vinegar to steam the spinach until slightly wilted, about 1 minute. Remove from heat and place on top of the salmon, spritzing with a squeeze of lemon juice.
In a separate bowl, combine the bread crumbs, parmesan, garlic powder, mustard powder, and minced basil. Sprinkle this over the spinach and tomatoes, then top with mozzarella.
Place into the warmed oven and change settings to broil. Broil on high for 3-4 minutes until the cheese has melted and the salmon is heated through.
Remove from the oven, drizzle with fancy traditional balsamic vinegar if desired, and serve with roasted vegetables and grains, or as part of a salad.
recipes.summary: No more meal-prep monotony, you'll love this Italian Caprese Salmon Recipe with tangy balsamic vinegar, blistered tomatoes, and melted mozzarella.

recipes.title: Keto Chicken Parm Recipe: Low Carb & Delicious
ingredients: 4-2 oz (12 oz total) chicken breasts or
4 tablespoons grated mozzarella cheese, finely grated
1 teaspoon oregano (sub an Italian seasoning if desired)
2 cups avocado oil (or neutral oil) for shallow-frying
Step One: Heat Oil, Butterfly, and Season Chicken
One chicken at a time, butterfly the breast by cutting crosswise down the middle. Season both sides with kosher salt and black pepper. Repeat with all four chicken breasts. Keep aside in a container.
Step Two: Follow an A-typical 'Breading' Method

In a shallow pan, heat the oil over a low flame/heat. The goal is to bring the oil to the ideal pan-frying temperature without going over the oil's smoke point. Keep a close eye on the oil, use an oil thermometer or laser thermometer to monitor and heat to 400F (200-C).
Avocado's smoke point is 520F (271 C); aside from the neutral flavor, the oil has high smoke point makes it ideal for frying and cooking.
As the oil reheats, coat the chicken pieces (aka 'breading' method) via the following procedure:
In three separate shallow bowls place:
the eggs, cracking and beating them, lightly seasoned with salt and pepper
the parmesan, mozzarella, and spices in a third bowl, tossing to incorporate and spread the spices in the cheese mix.
Season each bowl with a hint of salt and pepper and follow this pattern: flour, egg, cheese.

Working with one butterflied chicken breast at a time, coat the chicken in almond flour by firmly pressing both sides against the flour. Shake to remove excess and transfer to the egg mixture. Fully submerge, remove, and shake off any excess to then again dip a third time in the seasoned cheese mix, once again pressing firmly to coat both sides evenly.
Repeat with all four breasts and hold on a foil-lined sheet pan until needed.
Step Three: Pan Fry Chicken Breast and Prepare Romesco

Carefully drop chicken breasts into the hot oil by gently laying the chicken breast away from you (this will prevent any potential hot oil spills from hitting you). Fry the chicken breasts one or two at a time - depending on the size of your pan.
Pan-fry for 1.5-2 minutes per side, or until coating is golden brown. Avoid burning and adjust time and heat as needed.

Transfer all chicken breasts to the previously used foil-lined sheet pan. Transfer to the preheated oven and cook for an additional 6-8 minutes, or until the internal temperature of the chicken is 165F (74 C). Remove from the oven and let rest for 2-3 minutes before serving.
If meal prepping for the week, immediately cool down in the fridge in an open container/tupperware.
or gather it if you have it ready.
Make the romesco sauce ahead of time and keep it in your fridge as part of your
high fat & heart-healthy sauces meal prep
rotation. Or feel free to buy a prepared romesco/red pepper sauce from the store. Note the macronutrients will be different from the one displayed here if so.
Step Four: Cool, Portion, Sauce, and Store

Once all the ingredients are ready and cooled down, add 1 tablespoon of Romesco sauce on top of each chicken breast, along with a generous sprinkle of parmesan cheese. Spread romesco across the entire chicken breast, and add an extra tablespoon if desired for extra fat and flavor.
Transfer to a large Tupperware, and store covered in the fridge.
Now you have four delicious proteins meal prepped and ready to be reheated at any point of the week!

In the oven bake for 5 minutes, and then broil for 1-2 minutes; this will caramelize the cheese sprinkled on top of the sauce. The first step will heat the entire chicken breast without burning the outside, and the second step will give it color and an extra layer of flavor.
In an Air-fryer following the machine's instructions. I suggest you rotate the chicken breast halfway through while keeping a close eye on it to avoid the top from burning.
In the microwave cover with a damp paper towel and reheat for 1.5 minutes.
Store in the fridge for up to a week. Serve with your favorite
. The great thing about this dish is that you have your protein and your sauce all in one place.

instructions: Preheat oven to 400F (200C). One chicken at a time, butterfly the breast by cutting crosswise down the middle. Season both sides with kosher salt and black pepper.
In a shallow pan, slowly heat the avocado oil over a low flame/heat to 400F-450F (204C - 232C).

Crack eggs into a bowl, and mix. Add almond flour in a separate bowl. In a third shallow bowl, combine the mozzarella, Parmesan, and spices. 'Bread' the chicken breasts by coating in almond flour first, then egg, then seasoned cheese mix. Keep in a foil-lined sheet pan.

recipes.title: Keto Sheet Pan Eggs & Sausage Recipe
ingredients: 1/3 cup milk, full fat or cream (or coconut milk)
1 teaspoon of both kosher salt and black pepper
4 favorite sausage link, sliced into half-moons
1 avocado, sliced lengthwise, divided into quarters (optional)
Step One: Crack eggs, add Whites, Season, and Blend

Crack eggs directly into a blender or bowl (crack eggs into a bowl if you are using an immersion blender. Otherwise do this in the blender). Add the egg whites, milk, coconut oil, and season with salt, pepper, and caraway seeds.
Blend for 10-20 seconds, avoiding over blending, until all ingredients are emulsified.
Step Two: Prepare Pan, Cut Sausage, and Bake

Liberally spray the bottom of the sheet pan with spray oil. Add foil on top, covering the entire length and width of the pan, and spray liberally a second time. Slice the sausage links into half-moons and keep them aside in a bowl until needed (though the image shows parchment paper, upon testing the recipe, I realized foil was a better lining product than parchment, due to its ability to stick to the pan uniformly).
Pour the egg mix into the pan carefully. Add the sliced sausages by spreading around the pan as evenly as possible.

Transfer the pan carefully into the middle rack oven and bake for 30 minutes. Rotate the pan 180 degrees halfway (after 15 minutes) through the baking period - this will allow even cooking. Set a timer.
Step Three: Test, Cool, Cut, Serve, or Store

After 30 minutes, test the eggs by sticking a fork, toothpick, or cake tester into the middle of the pan and removing it. If eggs are fully set and none stick to your poking tool of choice, the sheet pan eggs are done. Otherwise, add 2-4 minutes at a time. Remove from the oven and cool on the side (set the pan on top of oven mitts or cooling racks to avoid cracking/burning the surface of your countertop).
Once cooled down, cut into even squares. The recipe makes 8 - 6.5oz/188g squares. Transfer to a deep Tupperware container and store in the fridge.
When ready to serve, top each square with sliced avocado, and a dollop of ricotta cheese. You won't regret it.
Do not over blend the egg mix

. Blend egg mix for about 10-20 seconds, just enough to have all ingredients come together. Over blending will cause the egg mix to foam excessively (as air gets introduced to the mix while blending).
If you have an immersion mixer (one of those handy stick blenders), I highly suggest you use that instead of a blender.
Coat the sheet pan excessively and multiple times with spray oil.

Spray and coat the bottom of the pan liberally, add the foil or parchment paper on top, and then spray the paper or foil once again liberally with spray oil. This will help you immensely when removing the cooked egg from the pan itself.
Use a rimmed 1" deep baking sheet, and if possible, non-stick.
The 1" rims will help maintain the egg in the pan and form a beautiful baked product. Ideally, the size of the sheet pan is 9x13. Other larger sizes will simply make the Keto Sheet Pan thinner.

Before completely removing it from the oven and letting it cool, poke the middle of the egg with a toothpick, fork, or cake tester. If no egg gets attached to your poking tool of choice, then you can confidently remove the sheet pan from the oven and begin to cool. We do this because as it is with most things, the middle is always the last part to cook - the rims will cook first and the fastest, always.

A neat little trick is to flip the sheet pan upside down on a cutting board after it's cooled down and pops it out of the pan. Be swift, and careful to avoid breaking the egg apart. Remove foil or paper and then cut and portion on the cutting board.

The avocado slices and ricotta garnish work incredibly well for this recipe. However, as I stated at the beginning of the blog, you can simply cook this keto recipe, cut into 6.5 squares and use for any meal prep needs throughout the week. Feel free to add some of our Trifecta
Store in the fridge for up to a week, covered in a Tupperware with an airtight container.

recipes.title: Delicious Keto Breakfast Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: (if desired, use turkey bacon - see recipe)
Countertop small oven or regular oven
Step One: Gather and Warm-Up Ingredients + Slice the Avocado
Gather all your previously cooked and prepared ingredients. See the recipes for the

Slice your avocado by cutting the fruit down the middle vertically. Remove the pit carefully with the butt end of your knife and discard the pit. Divide the avocado into quarters by halving each avocado half into another halve.

Slice the avocados lengthwise still with the skin on from the left side to the right side of the avocado, cutting from the top to the bottom vertically multiple times. This should form thin long pieces of avocado.
Scoop the now sliced avocado with a spoon into a small plate, sprinkle with a dash of lemon juice, some salt and leave aside until needed. Repeat with all 4 quarters.

In a foil-lined baking sheet, set 8 keto 'cloud' bread and 4 cooked bacon strips. Set a timer for 3 minutes and reheat items in a preheated oven. Add 2-3 extra minutes if necessary and remove the bread if they are getting too toasty. Keep warm

Meanwhile, heat 4 teaspoons of coconut oil in a nonstick pan over medium-low heat. Once the oil is beginning to smoke and slightly shimmer, add all 4 egg bites and sear for 1 minute on each side. Adjust heat as needed to avoid burning the sides. The goal is to develop a beautiful golden brown sear while reheating the egg bite from the outside to the inside.
By this time your bacon and bread should be warm. Remove from the oven if you haven't done so already.

In your working station, line up the bottom 4 keto bread in a line. Add 1/4 seasoned avocado on top of each keto bread, and place 1 egg bite on top of each avocado bread. Break or cut each bacon in half and top the now two halves of bacon on top of each keto egg bite. Top the sandwich with the other cloud bread and serve immediately.

If you are making these ahead of time for your meal prep, then my suggestion is to build the sandwiches cold and leave the avocados out until you are ready to eat them. Warm the sandwich in an airfryer or countertop oven for ease, add the avocado, and enjoy.

Store these sandwiches in a deep Tupperware or airtight container. If you want to simply prepare them each morning, store all the ingredients separated and build the sandwich whenever you are ready to eat. Reheat as indicated above. Store for no more than 5 days.
