4 tablespoons pomegranate seeds, store-bought to save time
¼ cup Trifecta almonds, favorite kind
4 tablespoon Mustard Honey Vin, store-bought or home-made
instructions: Prepare all ingredients as indicated. In a big bowl, mix all greens together. Season with a sprinkle of kosher salt.

Portion 0.5 cup of green mix in each plate, ⅓ cup of quinoa, 4 ounces of diced chicken, sprinkle 1 tablespoon of pom seeds, followed by 1 tablespoon of almonds, and 1 tablespoon of crumbled feta cheese in the middle of the salad.
Dress the salad with 1 tablespoon of mustard honey vin. Taste for seasoning and add another sprinkle of salt to the salad to bring up the flavor of all the ingredients. Enjoy!
Conversely, feel free to mix each salad in a bowl following the same serving sizes.
recipes.summary: This super beautiful, simple, healthy and delicious salad is a great option for lunch, using in-season fall and winter ingredients!

recipes.title: Fast Chicken Shawarma with Sweet Potato Recipe
ingredients: Garnish, optional: Greek-lemon yogurt and chopped fresh parsley
½ teaspoon of the following: ground garlic, ground cumin, ground caraway, ground cardamom, ground ginger, paprika, ground cinnamon, and black pepper
instructions: Mix the Shawarma wet rub ingredients in a bowl. The consistency should be a paste
Dice the chicken into bite-size squares, as even as possible. Add diced chicken into the bowl with the spice paste. Mix to coat evenly.

Heat up a non-stick pan over medium heat, and spray to coat with spray oil. Add the chicken and heat for 2-3 minutes. Taste to make sure chicken is heated through. Turn off heat and keep warm until needed.
Warm-up both sweet potato mash and broccoli in separate bowls for 2.5 minutes in the microwave. Add 30 seconds if broccoli and sweet potato are not heated through. Keep warm until needed.
Plate ½ cup of sweet potato, along with ½ cup of broccoli and 4 ounces of the chicken Shawarma. Garnish with greek-lemon yogurt and parsley if desired.
recipes.summary: The beauty behind chicken Shawarma is in the mix of spices. Follow the quick Shawarma wet rub recipe that will pack a punch of flavor and create an almost-authentic dish!

recipes.title: Korean Beef Bowl with Kimchi Recipe
instructions: Warm the beef patties in a microwave for about a minute. Then break them apart with your hands and set aside.
Chop scallions into small pieces, separating out 2 to 3 tablespoons of green ends for garnish.
In a small mixing bowl, combine liquid aminos, honey, vinegar and water.
Add sesame oil to a large saute pan and warm over medium-high heat.
Add in scallions and saute until onions cook through and start to brown.
Add shredded carrots to the pan and saute for another 1 to 2 minutes to soften.

Then add your beef crumble and sauce mixture, stirring everything together to mix well. Season with chili flakes and let cook for 3 to 5 minutes until the sauce begins to thicken and get absorbed by the beef mixture.
While your beef cooks, warm your rice in a microwave for 2 minutes.
Plate your bowl starting with the rice. Top with beef mixture and kimchi, and garnish with fresh scallions.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Crumbled Trifecta beef patties tossed in a spicy, sweet and savory Korean-style sauce, served over white rice with a crunchy and sour punch of kimchi. Ready in less time than it takes to call for delivery.

recipes.title: Vegan Pesto Recipe
instructions: Lightly roast the pistachios at 425 for 3-5 minutes, checking oven constantly. Remove from the oven and let cool slightly.
In a food processor, combine garlic, toasted pistachios, and 2 tablespoons olive oil. Process scraping sides as needed, until a paste is formed.
Add basil and mint to the food processor and pulse to incorporate basil into the paste.
Add lemon juice and process one last time, scraping the sides again as needed.
Remove basil paste into a bowl and fold remaining olive oil into the pistachio paste using a spatula.
Sprinkle a pinch of salt and mix to incorporate. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Creamy, nutty, and rich tasting, this vegan pesto sauce will add flavor and healthy fats to your weekly meal prep. Ready in less than 10 minutes!

recipes.title: Trifecta Five Minute Burger Recipe
ingredients: 4 hamburger buns, whole wheat or whole grain, toasted
2 tablespoons chipotle mayo or favorite condiment
½ cup arugula or any other bitter leafy green
½ cup Trifecta portobello mushroom, warmed
1 cup favorite Trifecta veggies, as a side
instructions: Begin by toasting buns in a toaster or toaster oven until golden brown.

Microwave Trifecta patties for 2 minutes to reheat, by making a small hole in a corner to allow steam to escape. Carefully remove plastic film and keep warm until needed. Spread the chipotle mayo on both burger buns. On the bottom bun, add a small ⅛ packed cup of arugula.

Slice tomatoes. Slice the avocados lengthwise and separate them into quarters. Fan avocado and place on top of the leafy greens. Carefully transfer the patties from the microwave to the bottom burger bun. Place 3 thin tomato slices or 2 thick slices on top of each patty. Top with ⅛ cup warm mushrooms and place the top bun on the burger.
Optional side: Reheat 1 cup of your favorite Trifecta vegetable and enjoy your 5-minute dinner!
recipes.summary: Looking for a simple quick meal using Trifecta's beef patties? Here's a legitimately simple and quick recipe. Enjoy and indulge in a healthy burger recipe!

recipes.title: Romesco Sauce Recipe
instructions: Roast the tomato (unpeeled) and pepper at 425°F for 20 minutes.
While your tomato and pepper roast, roast almonds and garlic on a separate sheet pan for 3 to 5 minutes.
Let cool and then remove the skin from tomato and peel the garlic.
In a food processor add your almonds, peeled tomato, roasted red pepper, olive oil, paprika, cayenne pepper, and vinegar.
Season with salt and cayenne pepper to taste.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Rich, velvety and smoky, this popular Spanish sauce will add so much flavor to any dish.

recipes.title: Chipotle Carne Surtida Beef Tacos Recipe
ingredients: 8 ounces or 2 Trifecta beef patties, crumbled
8 ounces or 2 Trifecta bison patties, crumbled
4 tablespoon reduced sodium soy sauce or tamari
1 tablespoon chipotle peppers in adobo, canned, pureed
Step One: Blend the Sauce and Prepare the 'Carnes'
In a blender combine the sauce ingredients, and blend for 30-45 seconds, until fully combined. The consistency should be thick, not watery.
Remove Trifecta beef and bison patties from the tray and crumble cold with your hands into a microwave safe bowl.
Step Two: Combine Carnes and Sauce. Reheat. Serve
Pour blended sauce into the bowl with beef and coat evenly by mixing with a spoon. Microwave for a minute and a half covered with a damp paper towel. Remove from microwave carefully.

If desired, this can also be reheated on the stove by spraying a saute pan with oil and adding the carne surtida beef. Heat over medium high heat stirring constantly for 2-3 minutes. Reduce heat to medium low and cook until half of the sauce is reduced and thickened up. Turn off the heat immediately and keep warm on the side. Set a timer, you don’t want to boil the sauce too long to the point of complete evaporation.

Meanwhile, warm up the tortillas by either using the microwave and reheat them wrapped in damp cloth for 30-45 seconds, or by reheating them over an open flame or on a hot non stick pan, quickly turning the tortillas on each side to warm them through. Keep them warm together wrapped in a napkin cloth.

Place two tortillas next to each other on a plate, with opposite ends holding opposite sides up. Serve 2 ounces of the carne surtida on each tortilla. Top with 1 tablespoon of cotija cheese and garnish with cilantro, pickled red onions, and sliced fresh sweet or fresno peppers.
Serve these tacos with a side of your favorite mixed
, or if you want substitute both of these with some

Every day that finishes with the letter "y" has been attributed as fitting for tacos. So to help you meet such a high demand of days per year (if you haven't made the math, that's 365 days), here's a quick recipe that can be interchanged with whatever ground protein your heart desires. Enjoy!
8 ounces or 2 Trifecta beef patties, crumbled
8 ounces or 2 Trifecta bison patties, crumbled
4 tablespoon reduced-sodium soy sauce or tamari
1 tablespoon chipotle peppers in adobo, canned, pureed
instructions: In a blender combine the sauce ingredients, and blend. The consistency should be thick, not watery. If too watery, add ¼ extra onion.

Remove Trifecta beef and bison patties from the tray and crumble cold with your hands into a microwave-safe bowl. Pour blended sauce into the bowl with beef and coat evenly by mixing with a spoon. Microwave for a minute and a half. Remove from the microwave carefully.

If desired, this can also be reheated on the stove by spraying a saute pan with oil and adding the carne surtida beef. Heat over medium-high heat stirring constantly for 2-3 minutes. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook until half of the sauce is reduced and thickened up. Turn off the heat immediately and keep warm on the side. Set a timer, you don’t want to boil the sauce too long to the point of complete evaporation.

Meanwhile, warm up the tortillas by either using the microwave and reheat them wrapped in a damp cloth for 30-45 seconds, or by reheating them on an open flame or on a hot non-stick pan, quickly turning the tortillas on each side to warm them through. Keep them warm together wrapped in a napkin cloth.

Place two tortillas next to each other on a plate, with opposite ends holding one side of the tortillas up. Serve 2 ounces of the carne surtida on each tortilla. Top with 1 tablespoon of cotija cheese and garnish with cilantro, pickled red onions, and sliced fresh sweet or Fresno peppers.
recipes.summary: Our chipotle tacos recipe makes a great lunch or dinner for any day. Use this sauce with beef or your favorite protein and dress your tacos to your liking!

recipes.title: Hunan Chicken Bowl Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups Trifecta brown or white rice
6 dried chilis, halved lengthwise, seeded, Sichuan or Thai whole
2 teaspoon Sherry Vinegar or red wine vinegar
¼ cup Soy sauce, low sodium, or tamari for gluten-free
instructions: Dice chicken into even square size bites. Combine broccoli and bell peppers in a bowl and in another separate bowl, portion your choice of Trifecta rice. Lastly combine all sauce ingredients except for the ginger, in a fourth bowl and whisk. Set aside.

In a non-stick pan, heat up 1 teaspoon of avocado oil over medium-high heat until slightly smoking and oil is shimmering. Add ginger and cook for a few seconds until fragrant, mixing constantly. Add the chicken and vegetables. Sautee until heated through for about 2-3 minutes.

Add sauce to pan and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon or spatula, and remove once the sauce has thickened. Taste for seasoning and adjust if needed. Feel free to add 1 teaspoon of honey if too savory.
Meanwhile, as the sauce thickens, reheat the rice in the microwave for 2 minutes.
Plate ½ cup of rice in a bowl, followed by around 5 ounces of the Hunan chicken. Garnish with fresh-picked cilantro and/or toasted sesame seeds. Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Hunan's cuisine has flavors that are dry and spicy. Find a bright dish with a sauce recipe that coats your chicken beautifully and satiates any spice cravings!

recipes.title: Curried Cauliflower Quinoa Recipe
instructions: Dice the scallion and bell pepper into small bite sized pieces. Then wash, de-stem and mince the parsley.
In a large mixing bowl, combine quinoa, cauliflower and chopped veggies. Add in raisins and parsley.
Stir in curry powder to evenly coat everything. Add some chili flakes to increase spiciness.
Add olive oil and lemon juice, and season with salt to taste.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Deep, rich and flavorful, this easy quinoa side dish is ready in less than 5 minutes. Pair with chicken or fish for macro balanced meal prep.

recipes.title: Beef Pita Pockets Recipe
ingredients: 16 ounces or 4 Trifecta beef patties, crumbled
1/2 cup sliced cucumber, deseeded, seasoned (see the method of preparation) +½ cup diced for Tzatziki sauce
1/2 cup Tzatziki sauce, store-bought or homemade
recipes.summary: This Mediterranean beef is simple and quick. It uses staples that pack a punch of flavor and make a make-shift hot pocket that is healthy and delicious.

recipes.title: Pineapple-Papaya Salsa Recipe
instructions: Dice pineapple and papaya into small pieces, equal in size. Set aside.
Mince shallots, cilantro, parsley and combine with oil in a small bowl. Set aside.
Peel and grate fresh turmeric and ginger root into the fruit mix. Add in black pepper and mix together.
Once ready to enjoy, mix the fruit and shallot-herb mix and serve!
recipes.summary: Make this sweet, tangy, and fresh fruit salsa in less than ten minutes to add some pep to your weekly meal prep.

recipes.title: 20 Minute Thai Beef Rice Bowl Recipe
ingredients: 16 ounces or 4 Trifecta beef patties, crumbled
3 tablespoons low sodium soy sauce, low sodium tamari, or liquid aminos
instructions: In a saucepan, quickly bring fish sauce, sesame oil, honey, lime juice, low sodium tamari/soy sauce, and sambal oelek up to a boil, reduce to simmer slightly, and mix to incorporate all ingredients. All ingredients should come together in 3 minutes tops. If the sauce is too loose, simmer for an extra 2 minutes, but avoid simmering down the sauce excessively to a thick syrup. We want a sauce that will coat the protein evenly. Think of the body of running maple, just slightly loser in the body.
Crumble the beef patties with your hands and add them to the saucepan with the simmering sauce. Add the carrots and Trifecta broccoli as well.
Cook for 3 minutes or until completely warmed through. Keep warm, avoiding the bottom from burning and adjusting heat as needed. If sauce becomes too thick, add a splash of stock or water.
Warm rice and divide into four bowls by plating ⅓ cup on each.

Plate 4-5 ounces of beef and sauce mixture on each plate. You may have a residual beef mix, as the volume increases when carrots and broccoli are added. Distribute amongst the four bowls evenly. Garnish with sesame seeds, chives, and scallions if desired!
recipes.summary: Inspired in Thai flavors, this versatile quick bowl recipe is set to satisfy your hunger cravings and meet your caloric needs!

recipes.title: Chimichurri Sauce Recipe
ingredients: The quality of your ingredients is key for this recipe. Opt for fresh herbs and seasonings to keep the flavors vibrant!
Step One: Prepare your Shallots and Oil-Vinegar Mix
Mince your shallots and garlic and combine them in a container. Add the red pepper flakes.
In the same bowl, add the oil and vinegar and mix to fully incorporate.
Step Two: Prepare and Cut your Fresh Herbs
Wash and pat dry your fresh herbs. Remove most of the thick portion of the bottom stems. Slice as thin and evenly as possible (chiffonade) and transfer to a separate bowl or container.
Step Three: Combine when Ready to Use!

Whenever you’re ready to use the sauce, mix the shallot and herb mix together in a separate third separate bowl, only using the amount you’ll need for that serving. The herbs should keep fresh in the fridge for a week. Feel free to add a small amount of oil to the herbs - this will preserve them further.
Season with black pepper to taste.
Tips From the Chef for Use and Storage
Adding the vinegar too early can cause your herbs to brown and makes the appearance of the chimichurri dull.
If you plan on using this sauce throughout the week, store your shallots, pepper flakes, oil, and vinegar in a separate container from the fresh herbs. Then when ready blend everything together.
This sauce pairs perfectly with most proteins - especially chicken and steak! Here are some of our favorite ways to use it:
in it and serve with stir-fry veggies over rice.
Learn how to cut carbs and burn more fat with this free
. Your new game plan for starting keto, complete with a keto macro meal planner and approved food lists.

Vibrant, tangy, and with just enough garlic, this simple sauce is a great way to take your meal prep up a notch. Ready in less than five minutes, make ahead of time and enjoy with Trifecta proteins all week long!
instructions: Mince shallots, garlic, and chili and combine in a bowl. Add oil and vinegar.
In a separate bowl chop all herbs and combine them together. Do not mix with the shallots.
In a separate bowl, mix the shallot mix with the herbs, in a 1.5 to 1 ratio of shallot mix to herbs. Mix together and enjoy!
recipes.summary: Make this vibrant, tangy, fresh, and garlicky sauce in less than five minutes. Our chimichurri sauce recipe will take your meal prep to the next level.

recipes.title: Vegan Savory Grilled Tofu, Broccoli, and Rice Bowl
ingredients: 1 can black beans, drained and rinsed
instructions: Evenly slice your tofu into strips (about 2 ounces each), pat dry and season with half of the nutritional yeast and pepper.
Add cooking spray on a non-stick grill pan and warm to medium-high heat. Once ready, add your tofu and grill for 3 minutes on each side until it starts to crisp up and leave grill marks.
Add your rice, drained beans, and broccoli to a large mixing bowl. Season with oil, remaining nutritional yeast, black pepper, and salt.
Portion out your rice mixture onto four plates or meal prep containers and top each with 3 pieces of grilled tofu.
Garnish with herbs and a sprinkle of salt.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Organic tofu seasoned with nutty and savoury nutritional yeast, grilled to perfection and served on top a bed of brown rice, black beans, and fresh veggies, tossed in a simple vinaigrette. Packed with plant-based protein and good nutrition.

recipes.title: Loaded Mediterranean Beef Bowl Recipe
ingredients: 1 cup sliced cucumber, deseeded, seasoned (see the method of preparation)
Parsley leaves, fresh chopped dill, feta cheese, red pepper flake
instructions: Make the greek yogurt sauce by combining ingredients in a bowl. Season with salt and taste. Adjust as needed. Transfer to a squeeze bottle if you have one. It will make the plating that much prettier.

Slice cucumbers into half-moons by slicing cut lengthwise and scooping all seeds out with a spoon. Discard seeds. Slice into even half-moons and transfer to a bowl. You can use a mandolin to make this process faster if you have one. Mix Trifecta cherry tomatoes with the cucumbers in the same bowl.
Season cucumbers-tomato mix with a splash of lime juice, olive oil, and salt. Taste for seasoning and adjust as needed. Keep on the side until plating time.

Microwave the Trifecta beef patties covered completely by a wet paper cloth towel, for a minute and a half. After the patties are slightly cooled down, crumble the patties to its original form - ground beef - with your hands into a bowl. Keep warm.

Warm-up quinoa in the microwave. In four bowls plate ⅓ cup of quinoa, followed by 4 ounces ground beef, ½ cup seasoned cucumber-tomato mix on the side, 1 tablespoon of hummus on another side, and top with greek yogurt by making long zig zags with a squeeze bottle, or simply spooning 1 tablespoon in the middle of the bowl. Make it as pretty as you’d like!
Garnish with red pepper flakes, parsley leaves, or fresh dill.
recipes.summary: Mediterranean flavors are clean and refreshing. This super simple recipe is a quick and tasty solution for any day of the week. Really. It's delicious.

recipes.title: Smoked Paprika Steak Stir Fry Recipe
instructions: Wash and chop your veggies and slice the steak into bite-sized pieces. Mince the fresh cilantro leaves and stems.
Using spray oil, coat a non-stick skillet and saute asparagus over medium-high heat for 2 to 3 minutes.
Add tomatoes and cook for another 2 minutes.
Add steak and cook for an additional minute, then turn off the heat.
Add sauce mixture to the pan and stir to coat everything evenly.
Plate it and dig in!
recipes.summary: The perfect match for any meal prep diet. This paleo and keto steak stir-fry recipe is smokey, savory and a little tangy, and takes less than 10 minutes to make!

recipes.title: Paleo Beef Sloppy Joe Recipe
ingredients: 16 ounces or 4 Trifecta beef patties, fully crumbled
4 each or 1 Tbsp. garlic cloves, minced
1 cup of stock, beef or vegetable
Kosher salt and black pepper, to taste
instructions: If you are using raw ground beef take it out from the fridge and transfer it to a bowl. Season the beef with salt and pepper and mix it in with your hands to fully incorporate the seasoning. If you are using the Trifecta burgers, process them on your food processor and keep them aside.

Prepare your aromatics by cutting the shallots, mincing the garlic, and grating the ginger all into one bowl. Keep aside until needed. Lastly, measure and mix all your spices into a separate small bowl and combine the maple syrup, Worcestershire sauce, and tomato sauce in another bowl. Keep tomato paste on the side as well. Keep your apple cider vinegar on the side.

Heat 1 tablespoon of avocado oil over medium-high heat in your pan until the oil is loose and shimmering. Add your aromatic mix and constantly mix to avoid burning. Reduce heat to medium - you want to hear a sear throughout this process.
Continue to cook until all aromatics are translucent and caramelized. Deglaze with apple cider vinegar.

As soon as your deglaze the pan, mix, and add your spice mix to the onions. Mix to fully incorporate. Over the same medium heat, toast the spices for 10-20 seconds, or until the smell is fragrant. Add tomato paste. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper. Add tomato sauce, Worcestershire, and maple syrup. Mix.
Immediately after, add ground beef and mix to fully incorporate the aromatic-spice mixture. Increase the heat to medium-high and begin cooking the ground meat. Stir and scrape any brown bits.
Once the ground beef has started to turn brown, add the stock bring to a boil, and then bring the mixture down to a simmer. Simmer for 5-10 minutes or until the liquid has reduced 85-90% of the way.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
recipes.summary: This super fun, easy, and flavor punched low carb paleo sloppy joe recipe is built with tons of flavor for your meal prep or weekly dinner needs1

recipes.title: Sriracha Honey Sesame Chicken Recipe
ingredients: ¾ cup water + 1 tablespoon garlic powder
¼ cup soy sauce, or tamari, low sodium
2 teaspoon cornstarch + 2 tablespoon water, mixed in a bowl
instructions: Place together all sauce ingredients in a medium pot and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to simmer and stir constantly for 2-5 minutes, allowing the sauce to slightly reduce. Add cornstarch 1 teaspoon at a time, allowing the cornstarch to react with the heat. Add a total of 3 teaspoons first and then adjust as desired. The sauce will thicken up over time. Cover and keep warm.
Dice chicken into bite-size. Add chicken to pot with sauce and mix to incorporate. Simmer for 2-3 minutes or until chicken is warmed and evenly covered. Cover pot and keep warm until needed.
Reheat the rice and broccoli for 3 minutes in two separate microwave-safe bowls. Add an extra 30 seconds to a minute if extra time is needed.
In a bowl, plate the following: ⅓ cup of reheated Trifecta brown rice, followed by 4 ounces of the sesame honey ginger chicken, and ½ cup of the reheated Trifecta broccoli.
Garnish with sesame seeds, picked fresh cilantro, extra Sriracha, and/or chives & scallions. Enjoy!
recipes.summary: A balance of spicy, sweet, and the slight yet unique taste of sesame makes this recipe a pantry-friendly favorite for your meal prep!

recipes.title: Keto Salmon Patties with Chimichurri Sauce Recipe
ingredients: 16 ounces Trifecta salmon, or fresh salmon, processed
Step One: Preheat Oven & Prepare Ingredients

Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and pull out your food processor. Dice your onion, and saute over medium heat in a saute pan by spraying it with oil. Mix constantly to avoid over-charring and add a splash of water as needed to help the onions caramelize and reduce heat to medium-low until onions are fully caramelized around 10-15 minutes.
Meanwhile, start by pulsing the flax seeds on the food processor until these are fully grounded. If you have already ground flaxseed, you can skip this step.
Transfer to a medium bowl. Chop your parsley and combine in the same bowl as the flax seeds. Hold on the side.

or raw salmon, you can follow these guidelines to make great salmon patties. Using your hands, break the salmon apart into smaller chunks and add it to the food processor. Pulse a few times until the salmon is broken down into a paste-like form.

Add the whole liquid eggs, granulated garlic, avocado oil, ground flaxseed, and chopped parsley into the food processor. Season with a pinch of black pepper and salt. Pulse a few more times to combine. Transfer to a bowl scraping all sides with a spatula.
Step Three: Make the Patties and Bake
Mold your salmon patties by weighing each to about 4.2 oz on a scale. Make them into a ball and then press them down flat to create a patty shape.

Transfer to a parchment-lined baking sheet. Bake your fish cakes for 15 minutes, followed by 5 minutes under the broiler to give them a nice golden crust. Remove from broiler and serve immediately or cool completely uncovered in the fridge before storing.
While your salmon patties cook you can take advantage of the time and make the chimichurri sauce.

A chimichurri sauce is a super simple and quick recipe you can make any day of the week. You can make it the classic way as displayed in the image below, or you can also simply combine all the ingredients in a blender and blend the sauce! This will create a bright and deep green sauce packed with tons of flavor. Note that if you do blend the sauce, over time, the green will become browner due to the vinegar presence.
Combine the cooked salmon cakes with 1 tablespoon of the chimichurri and serve!
for later, keep the shallots, pepper flakes, oil, and vinegar separated from the herbs until you're ready to eat.

You can also serve these with a side of tartar sauce, and lemon wedges to squeeze fresh lemon juice as part of the presentation. The sweet acidity combination of the lemon will mix well with that of the chimichurri, and will also create an Instagram-worthy meal!
To reheat salmon cakes, warm in the oven for 5 minutes at 350 degrees
Fahrenheit or pop in the microwave for a minute and a half (covered with a damp paper cloth towel). You can also crisp them back up in an air fryer or in a non-stick skillet!
Want to get more out of your weekly meal prep efforts? Stick to your
. Pre-cooked and ready to heat, these ingredients are organic, sustainably sourced, and minimally seasoned to maximize your meal prep.
Keto Salmon Patties with Chimichurri Sauce Recipe
Add some oomph and creamy, rich flavor to your Trifecta salmon with this simple salmon cakes recipe. Then top it all off with a zesty and vibrant chimichurri sauce!
16 ounces Trifecta salmon or fresh salmon
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and pull out your food processor. Dice your onion, and saute over medium heat in a saute pan by spraying it with oil. Mix constantly to avoid over-charring and add a splash of water as needed to help the onions caramelize and reduce heat to medium-low until onions are fully caramelized around 10-15 minutes. Meanwhile, start by pulsing the flax seeds on the food processor until these are fully grounded. If you have already ground flaxseed, you can skip this step. Transfer to a medium bowl. Chop your parsley and combine in the same bowl as the flax seeds. Hold on the side.

Whether you are using Trifecta's A La Carte Salmon or raw salmon, you can follow these guidelines to make great salmon patties. Using your hands, break the salmon apart into smaller chunks and add it to the food processor. Pulse a few times until the salmon is broken down into a paste-like form. Add the whole liquid eggs, granulated garlic, avocado oil, ground flaxseed, and chopped parsley into the food processor. Season with a pinch of black pepper and salt. Pulse a few more times to combine. Transfer to a bowl scraping all sides with a spatula.

Mold your salmon patties by weighing each to about 4.2 oz on a scale. Make them into a ball and then press them down flat to create a patty shape. Transfer to a parchment-lined baking sheet. Bake your fish cakes for 15 minutes, followed by 5 minutes under the broiler to give them a nice golden crust. Remove from broiler and serve immediately or cool completely uncovered in the fridge before storing.

While your salmon patties cook you can take advantage of the time and make the chimichurri sauce.A chimichurri sauce is a super simple and quick recipe you can make any day of the week. You can make it the classic way as displayed in the image below, or you can also simply combine all the ingredients in a blender and blend the sauce! This will create a bright and deep green sauce packed with tons of flavor. Note that if you do blend the sauce, over time, the green will become browner due to the vinegar presence. Combine the cooked salmon cakes with 1 tablespoon of the chimichurri and serve!
recipes.summary: Find a simple low-carb keto salmon patties recipe loaded with creamy, rich flavors and topped with a zesty, vibrant chimichurri sauce.

recipes.title: Macaroni and Beef Recipe
ingredients: 2 teaspoon grapeseed oil or preferred neutral oil
instructions: Cook pasta elbows as indicated by the box. Save ¼ cup of the starchy water.
Drain pasta over a colander and return elbow to the same pot. Sprinkle a small splash of olive oil into the pasta and mix to coat all elbows. Cover with lid and keep warm.
Crumble Trifecta Beef by hands into a bowl and keep on the side until needed.

In a large saute pan, heat oil over medium heat until the oil is slightly smoking, quickly for about 30 seconds, saute garlic and then immediately add the beef. Add tomato sauce, Worcestershire sauce, honey, broth. Mix to fully incorporate. Add the ¼ cup starchy water along with cherry tomatoes and the macaroni elbows. Mix, and bring to a light simmer, and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until warmed through. Taste for seasoning and adjust as needed. Be careful not to burn yourself when tasting food. Remove from heat if the sauce has thickened up. Keep warm until needed.
Get ready 4 plates. Carefully and evenly portion the macaroni and beef on the four plates.
Garnish with chives and cheese of preference. Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Macaroni and beef play a twist on a classic dish that everyone as a kid loved - add some veggies and make this recipe a healthy and hearty meal!

recipes.title: Easy Keto Buffalo Hot Sauce Recipe
ingredients: Step One: Measure, Mix, and Done!
In a medium bowl, large enough to hold all ingredients, combine the coconut oil, Frank's hot sauce, apple cider vinegar, and erythritol.
If using tabasco, add the tabasco along with garlic powder, paprika, black pepper, and cayenne pepper (if using). Whisk all ingredients in bowl to incorporate.
Taste for seasoning. Add small amounts of vinegar and keto sweetener if spice is too strong, or if flavor doesn't seem balanced.
Transfer sauce to your preferred storage container. Cover and store in fridge until needed.

The beautiful thing about this sauce is that you are in control of the spice level. I personally love spice that makes me cry... err, sweat. If you are more of a mild person, eliminate the cayenne pepper and only add a small dash of Tabasco.
If you want to add some smokiness, Tabasco has a Tabasco Chipotle line, that is (in my humble opinion) absolutely delicious. You can also add smoked paprika and that will do the trick.
Traditional buffalo sauce uses butter, but this recipe uses coconut oil to provides a beautiful layer of MCT's (
) for your keto diet. Feel free to substitute the coconut oil with butter, or do a 50/50 split for the best of both worlds.
If you don't like spice that much, use smoked paprika or regular paprika instead of cayenne pepper, and also eliminate the tabasco.
Store it in the fridge in an airtight container and it will last up to two weeks.
This sauce can be eaten hot or cold.
To reheat, toss it in small saucepan over low-medium heat and taste for seasoning - the salt level will diminish when the sauce is heated! If necessary, add a pinch of salt.

This sauce was created so you can use it in your meal prep. Make a x2 batch once and keep it in your refrigerator to whoop a quick lunch or dinner at any given point. Use the nutrition guidelines on the nutrition card below and feel free to use our
for a quick and delicious meal!
Learn how to cut carbs and burn more fat with this free
. Your new game plan for starting keto, complete with a keto macro meal planner and approved food lists.
We developed a fun and easy authentic buffalo flavor, piquant, yet beautifully balanced with the right amount of acid and sweet notes for your keto meal prep.
instructions: Melt your coconut oil if solid, and butter in the same pan over low heat. Measure all ingredients and combine in a medium bowl.
Whisk vigorously to dilute erythritol into liquid. Mix until all erythritol is fully mixed into the sauce.
Taste for seasoning, and adjust if desired. Transfer to container and store in the fridge as needed.
recipes.summary: Easy keto buffalo hot sauce recipe with authentic buffalo flavor, piquant, yet beautifully balanced with the right amount of acid and sweet notes.

recipes.title: Mahi Mahi Fish Taco Bowl
ingredients: 2 cups Green and purple cabbage, shredded
instructions: Add cabbage, carrots, and cilantro to mixing bowl and season with 1/2 of lime juice and a pinch of salt.
In another small bowl, blend yogurt, garlic, honey, 1/2 of the taco seasoning, and remaining lime juice. Add salt to taste.
Add yogurt mixture to veggies, stirring well to coat everything evenly.
Season pre-cooked fish with remaining taco seasoning and pan sear in 1 tablespoon of oil - about 1 to 2 minutes per side.
Warm brown rice in the microwave for 90 seconds while fish cooks.
To plate, layer ingredients starting with brown rice, followed by slaw, and fish, dividing ingredients evenly between four portions.
Top with pico de gallo and extra cilantro.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Easy fish taco bowls loaded with brown rice, a crunchy slaw tossed in a tangy, sweet and slightly spicy dressing, and topped with perfectly cooked fish and fresh salsa.

recipes.title: Drool Worthy Chicken Cobb Salad
ingredients: ½ cup (volume) romaine lettuce, rough chopped, washed
1 bacon slice, turkey, baked crunchy, crumbled
instructions: Prep your salad dressing ahead of time, blending all of the ingredients together in a mixer.
In a preheated small oven, bake bacon until crispy. Drain on a paper towel to drain any excess fat.
Dice chicken and egg into small even squares, and keep separate.
Slice cucumbers into half-moons and shred carrots on a coarse side of a cheese grater.
Wash and prepare mixed baby greens, romaine, and shredded cabbage. Mix greens together in a bowl. Add 1/2 tablespoon of dressing and a pinch of salt. Toss greens to coat evenly.
Plate your salad, greens first followed by remaining ingredients, except the blue cheese.
Sprinkle blue cheese across the top of the salad and drizzle with remaining dressing.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: A simple chicken cobb salad, made with lean chicken breast, crunchy veggies, crisp and salty bacon, and creamy blue cheese, all tossed in a zesty, tangy vinaigrette.

recipes.title: Matcha Fat Bombs Recipe
ingredients: 2/3 cup Protein powder (We use Thorne Vanilla)
1.5 Tbsp Tapioca Maltodextrin (50% of coconut oil)
Step One: Create the Matcha Coating

Start by taking your coconut oil from a solid to a liquid - basically all you need to do is heat it up (but don't hit it up too much)! This is best accomplished using a double boiler or a heat safe bowl placed over a pot of boiling water. Add the coconut oil to the top bowl and heat until it becomes a clear liquid. Then set about two teaspoons of oil aside for a later step. Set the oil aside somewhere warm, like on a warm stove, where it will stay liquid at room temperature.
If the oil starts to reset, pass it through a heat source one more time.
Using a spoon, add your maltodextrin to the remaining coconut oil until fully incorporated. (You can find

). Mix with a whisk and with your hands to break it all into a small powder-like consistency. The oil mixture should turn into a powder. For a finer powder texture, add in small amounts of maltodextrin at a time. Set aside.
Tapioca maltodextrin is a natural ingredient derived from Tapioca starch. This modern neutral-tasting ingredient is used to transform any oil into a powder.
Step Two: Mix Protein and Almond Butter

In a separate bowl, mix the almond butter, protein powder, and leftover coconut oil together until smooth. We recommend you use Thorne Vanilla Whey Protein powder (for flavor and safety purposes). Thorne's Vanilla Whey Protein has a slight amount of stevia which makes these bites slightly sweet and delicious.
Tip: For best results, use a nut butter with visible oil separation in the jar to add more moisture and creaminess to the balls.

Use a food scale to portion out the 'dough' - each ball should be approximately 24 grams. Roll the dough into small balls and set on a sheet tray lined with a silicon pad to prevent them from sticking. Also, if you have gloves, we highly recommend you use them. If not, you'll become Edward-Peanut-Hands.
Step Four: Coat Fat Bombs with Matcha Mix

When ready, roll each ball into the matcha mix to coat evenly with the bright green powder. Fell free to pop one in your mouth, just to taste it (aka baker's dozen). Oh, and one last thing... these have a small amount of caffeine. Yep. Because of the matcha. Caffeine in matcha - unlike coffee - doesn't have the 'crash' effect. Sustained energy galore here!

To let the balls fully set, store them in the fridge for a couple of hours or overnight until you’re ready to enjoy them! If storing, make sure they are tightly wrapped or portioned on individual ramekin containers or small Tupperware.

If you have small round containers, transfer the coated matcha bombs to them and cover. Refrigerate for up to two weeks. To keep the coconut oil-matcha coating intact, refrigerate and store in a cool place, preferably.
Learn how to cut carbs and burn more fat with this free
. Your new game plan for starting keto, complete with a keto macro meal planner and approved food lists.
Add these creamy, nutty, and slightly sweet fat bombs to your keto meal prep routine to help you crush your macros and tackle mid-day hunger.
1.5 Tbsp Tapioca Maltodextrin (50% of coconut oil)
2/3 cup Protein powder (We use Thorne Vanilla)
instructions: Melt coconut oil over a double boiler. Set aside 2 Tsp. and keep warm.
Using a spoon, add maltodextrin, and matcha powder into 3 Tbsp coconut oil liquid, and mix with until a powder texture is formed.

In a separate bowl combine the nut butter, protein powder, and 2 Tsp. coconut oil. Mix into a smooth dough-like consistency. If too wet, add a little extra protein powder until you get that kneaded-like-dough consistency, even and not runny looking.
Using a food scale, portion out the dough into 24-gram pieces and roll into small, smooth balls.
Set aside in a small cookie tray or plate.
Once all balls are rolled, coat each ball in the matcha powder.
Refrigerate for a couple of hours to let them set.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Doughy, flavor-packed, high-fat, and moderate-protein this keto matcha fat bombs recipe tastes even better than it looks.

recipes.title: Easy Paleo Chili Beef Recipe
ingredients: 2 cups diced tomato, canned, juice drained and ¼ cup reserved.
instructions: Microwave the Trifecta beef patties covered completely by a damp paper cloth towel, for a minute and a half. Remove from microwave and after the patties are slightly cooled down, crumble the patties to its original form - ground beef - with your hands into a separate bowl. Keep warm.
Combine all the seasoning spice mix ingredients in a separate bowl, with the exception of salt.

Dice the onion and keep in a bowl. Heat oil over medium-high in a pot large enough to hold all ingredients. Once the oil is shimmering and slightly smoking, add the onions, mix constantly to prevent onions from burning. Reduce heat to medium and cook onions for 3-5 minutes or until they start to develop a caramelized color. Use your best judgment on the timing. Once some caramelization begins to happen, add bell peppers and garlic and mix to cook for 2 minutes, avoiding the garlic from burning. Adjust heat as needed.

Add the spice mix to the pot and mix to fully coat all the vegetables. The point of this step is to bring the fragrant tones of the spices up. After a few seconds, add canned tomatoes by crushing them with your hand as you add them to the pot, or by crushing them with a wooden or metal spoon in the pot. Mix and scrape any brown bits from the pan. Add ¼ cup of tomato juice, plus water or stock and increase the heat to bring to a boil. Once boiling, reduce heat to medium-high and maintain an aggressive simmer.

Immediately add the ground beef along with honey, and mix to incorporate. Bring the pot back up to an aggressive simmer, mixing constantly, for about 3 minutes or until beef is heated through. Add extra water or stock if the sauce is becoming too thick.
Taste for seasoning and adjust as needed. Use your best judgment in the heat adjustment.
Portion around 5-6 ounces of chili into 4 bowls. There may be excess chili as the volume increases with the addition of all other ingredients (so you may have to weigh a bit more than 6 ounces).
Garnish each bowl with your preferred condiments or garnishes (chives, cheese, tortilla strips, and even fried shallots are some of my favorites).
recipes.summary: The rich combination of spices makes this paleo chili recipe deeply rich in flavor and a quick solution for your meal prep or a quick meal!

recipes.title: Tamarind-Chipotle Steak Nachos Recipe
ingredients: 4 ounces Trifecta flat iron steak, cut into bite-size squares
18 each baked blue corn tortilla chips
¼ cup green onions, white part only, sliced thin
½ teaspoon chipotle pepper puree, canned
1 fl ounce beef stock, low sodium
instructions: Add coconut oil into a saute pan over medium-high heat. Once the oil is slightly smoking add sliced green onions and saute until translucent. Add garlic and saute, mixing often to avoid the onion and garlic from burning.
Add the steak and incorporate it into the onion mix.
Add chipotle, tamarind, and honey and mix to coat steak.

Deglaze pan with stock and stir. Adjust heat as needed to avoid food from burning. Bring the pan to a gentle boil and reduce stirring often to create a coat with the sauce as it reduces. Once ready, remove from heat and keep warm.
Plate greens followed by tortilla chips, and cold broccoli. Top with steak, spreading throughout the plate, and garnish with green onion bulbs and cilantro.
Season with lemon juice.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Reinvent traditional nachos with a sweet and spicy steak sauce and plenty of green veggies - still giving you that crispy, chewy, savory experience you're looking for.

recipes.title: Beef Enchilada Recipe
ingredients: 16 each corn or flour tortillas, soft 4”-6”
2 cups enchilada sauce, store-bought or preferred enchilada sauce
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 F. Warm up the tortillas by either using the microwave and reheat them wrapped in napkin cloths for about a minute and a half at 75% of the microwave’s power, or by reheating them on an open flame or on a hot non-stick pan, quickly turning the tortillas on each side to warm them through and keeping them warm in a small bundle.

Microwave the Trifecta beef patties covered completely by a wet paper cloth towel, for a minute and a half. After the patties are slightly cooled down, crumble the patties to its original form - ground beef - with your hands into a bowl. Combine the Trifecta bell pepper with the beef and mix to incorporate. Keep aside.
Get a baking dish ready, by spraying a light coat of oil on all sides of the baking dish.

Set up a cutting board as your working station to roll the enchiladas. Grab the warm tortillas, beef and green bell pepper mix, enchilada sauce, and set up your station to be able to work in the following way:
A. Place one, two, or however many tortillas comfortably fit on your cutting board right next to each other, without overlapping.
B. Weigh and place 1.5 ounces of the beef+bell pepper mix on one end of the tortillas.

C. Roll the tortilla over the beef and create a semi-tight cylinder. Carefully transfer the tortillas to the sprayed baking sheet, and place the seam in the bottom to avoid the enchiladas from rolling open. Place them all back to back starting from one end of the baking sheet, to the other. Repeat with all 16 tortillas.

Pour the sauce on top of the enchiladas, and top with cheese. Place in the oven for about 7-10 minutes or until cheese is melted and enchiladas are warmed through. Carefully remove the pan from the oven and serve 4 enchiladas per plate. Garnish with cilantro. Enjoy!
recipes.summary: A few ingredients make up for a quick dinner for any day of the week. This classic Mexican dish is set to be a quick solution for any day of the week.

recipes.title: Teriyaki Beef & Rice Bowl Recipe
ingredients: Sesame seeds, sliced scallions, cilantro, or red pepper flakes for garnish. All optional.
instructions: Microwave the Trifecta beef patties covered completely by a wet paper cloth towel, for a minute and a half. After the patties are slightly cooled down, crumble the patties to its original form - ground beef - with your hands into a bowl. Keep warm.
In separate bowls microwave the brown rice and tomatoes. Air-fry/roast/blanch the Brussels Sprouts. Keep warm.

In four separate bowls, plate a rice base of ⅓ cup, followed by 4 ounces of the crumbled Trifecta beef, ½ cup of broccoli, ¼ cup of the carrot matchsticks, and 2 tablespoons of the Teriyaki sauce. Feel free to heat the sauce in a small saucepan beforehand.
Garnish with sesame seeds, scallions, cilantro, and pepper flakes. Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Bowls are incredibly functional and you can make them look as pretty as possible. This simple Teriyaki beef and rice bowl can be put together in no time.

recipes.title: Healthy Chicken Quesadillas Recipe
ingredients: 12 ounce Trifecta chicken, shredded or breast, diced
Red salsa (calories not accounted for in recipe card)
instructions: If using chicken breast, dice the chicken into small squares, as even as possible. Keep aside. Portion and mix the vegetables into a separate bowl and keep aside. Similarly, weigh and mix the cheddar and pepper jack cheese into a separate bowl.

Working with one tortilla at a time, lay the tortilla on a cutting board. Spread on one side the cheese mix, followed by about ⅓ cup of the vegetable mix, and 3 ounces of the Trifecta diced or shredded chicken. Make a flat as much as possible.
Fold the opposite end half of the tortilla on top of the side of the tortilla with all the quesadilla guts. Set aside in a tray until needed. Repeat the process with the remaining three tortillas.

Spray a large non-stick pan with some spray oil and heat over medium heat. Working with one or two at a time (depending on the size of the large skillet), transfer the quesadillas to the pan and toast for about 3-4 minutes on each side. Adjust heat as needed - you want a golden-brown color on both sides. If the quesadilla begins to burn, remove the pan from heat or flip the quesadilla to avoid the tortilla from charring. Keep a close eye and judge according to color and how fast the cheese melts.
Repeat with all quesadillas, remove from pan and let stand for 1 minute. Cut each quesadilla into four equal triangles. Serve with your favorite salsa or as is.
recipes.summary: Choose your favorite Trifecta proteins, veggies, and add your cheese mix of choice to create a super quick and delicious quesadilla!

recipes.title: Buffalo Chicken Meal Prep Recipe
ingredients: ½ cup Frank’s Red Hot sauce
instructions: Melt the butter in a medium saucepan over medium-low heat. Once fully melted, add the rest of the ingredients in the pot. Mix, taste, and adjust for seasoning if needed (unlikely as Frank’s is pretty salty as is). Bring to a gentle boil and let sauce slightly reduced.

Dice chicken into even bite-sized squares. Transfer chicken into the pot with sauce and bring up to heat until chicken is fully heated through. Reduce heat to low and keep covered until needed. Stir every now and then if for some reason you keep the pot on the fire for longer than 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat up Trifecta mashed potato and cauliflower in two separate microwave-safe bowls for 3 minutes. Add 30 seconds if the food is not heated all the way through.

As an idea: Plate ½ cup of mashed potatoes in each plate, topped by 4 ounces of chicken buffalo and ½ cup of the heated cauliflower. Feel free to sprinkle any leftover buffalo sauce on the cauliflower.
recipes.summary: This healthy buffalo recipe can be cross utilized for any meal prep need! Use this recipe as your guideline and have fun making any buffalo-inspired recipe

recipes.title: Vegetarian Sweet Potato and Greens Breakfast Hash Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups thinly chopped collard greens, dandelion greens, spinach, or kale
Medium skillet or cast iron pan
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add two tablespoons of coconut oil. Add in the small diced sweet potato and cook for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally. The potatoes will start to brown and crisp up. While the potatoes are cooking, move on to step 2.
Remove the tofu from its packaging and crumbIe it into a small bowl. Add the nutritional yeast, onion powder, garlic powder, black pepper, paprika, and pinch of salt. Mix to combine then set aside.

Once the potatoes start to get tender and browned, add in the diced bell pepper and greens if you are using a hearty green like collard greens or kale. Saute the potatoes and peppers for 3 minutes. If you are using any other roasted vegetables, add them right now as well. If you are using leafy greens such spinach, dandelion greens, or arugula wait to add those at the very end, after the tofu, as they will cook extremely fast. If the potatoes and peppers start to stick to the bottom of the skillet or pan, add a tiny amount of water or vegetable stock to prevent the ingredients from burning; this is called deglazing the pan.
Add the tofu and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally until the tofu is firm and cooked through. Season with salt and pepper, and a squeeze of lemon. Then, dig in!
recipes.summary: This one-pan vegetarian sweet potato and greens breakfast hash recipe will provide 4 days of breakfast in less than 30 minutes.

recipes.title: Quick Shredded Chicken Sliders Recipe
instructions: Transfer the pulled chicken from the Trifecta tray to a bowl big enough to comfortably hold the chicken and be able to mix it all.

Add all ingredients except the vegetables into the same bowl with the chicken. Mix with hands (preferably wearing gloves) until chicken and ingredients are fully incorporated. Taste for seasoning and add salt if needed. Keep on the side until needed.
Toast buns until golden brown. Meanwhile, reheat your favorite Trifecta veggies in a microwave-safe bowl.

Plate 1 ounce of chicken per slider, top with minced chives or scallions, and place the top slider bun on top of the chicken mix. Plate ½ cup of vegetables next to the sliders and enjoy a delicious quick meal.
recipes.summary: Using either of Trifecta's chicken breast or shredded chicken, this quick and simple lunch can be made at any point to satisfy that craving for sliders!

recipes.title: Salmon Stir-Fried Noodles Recipe
instructions: Place egg noodles (dry/dehydrated) into a pan of boiling water along with a pinch of salt, and cover for 3-4 minutes.
Remove the noodles from the heat and drain. Keep the noodles in the sieve on top of the pan, and place the lid back over so that the steam keeps the noodles from drying out.
Quarter bok choy lengthwise (cutting it in half, and then repeating the process with each half).
Slice carrot into long, thin strips, about a quarter-inch thick. And chop broccoli into 1-inch long pieces.
Warm pan over medium heat. Coat with spray oil and add vegetables. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
For the sauce, add low sodium soy sauce, hot sauce, and corn starch to 2/3 cups of water and mix well.
Using a garlic press, crush the garlic cloves into the mixture and stir.
Add sauce to the stir-fry vegetables in the pan and reduce heat to a simmer for several minutes, stirring and letting the sauce reduce a bit.
Turn off the heat and mix in the noodles, coated everything evenly with the sauce.
Transfer all contents of the pan to a plate and then break apart chunks of the Trifecta Salmon and arrange on top. Then Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Perfect your clean bulk with healthy fats, quality carbs, and nutrient-dense proteins, with the easy salmon stir-fry recipe from Rob Riches. Ready in less than 15 minutes!

recipes.title: Vegan Roasted Veggie Sandwich Recipe
ingredients: 2 slices of thick, whole grain bread
instructions: Toast the bread.
Smear hummus on both slices of bread.
Top with veggies.
Put it all together.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Keep meal prep simple with this easy vegan roasted veggie sandwich. Hearty whole grain bread and creamy hummus piled high with roasted veggies.

recipes.title: Thai Peanut Chicken Meal Prep Recipe
ingredients: 16 ounce Trifecta chicken breast, small diced
2 teaspoon brown sugar or 1 ¼ teaspoon erythritol
1 tablespoon Soy sauce, low sodium, or tamari for gluten-free
1 teaspoon chili garlic, like Sambal Oleke
instructions: In a bowl, mix all the sauce ingredients. Whisk to incorporate. Conversely, if desired, add all ingredients into a food processor and pulse 3-4 times. Transfer to an airtight container.
Dice chicken and transfer to a large mixing bowl. Add carrots. Pour the sauce on top of the chicken and mix to coat.
Warm a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Spray with oil and once the pan is hot and oil is shimmering and beginning to slightly smoke, add the chicken coated in the sauce.

Sauté 2-3 minutes, adjusting the heat as needed, until warmed through. Taste a piece of chicken to see if the chicken is ready. Serve in a plate with favorite sides, portioning about 5 ounces of chicken mix per plate.
Season with peanuts, almonds, sesame seeds, cilantro, and/or chili flakes.
recipes.summary: Creamy peanut butter combined with coconut milk creates a delicious sauce for a Thai inspired dish for any day!

recipes.title: Vegan High Protein Veggie Pasta Recipe
instructions: Cook pasta al dente. Let cool and set aside.
Dice scallions and coarsely chop parsley for garnish. Set aside.
Combine pasta and veggies in a large bowl.
Add oil, lemon zest, and seasoning.
Finish with parsley and scallion.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: This vegan pasta recipe is simple, refreshing and hearty. Made with lentil pasta with fresh cooked organic veggies and vibrant herbs in a salty and savory dressing.

recipes.title: Simple Quinoa Bowl Recipe
instructions: Warm quinoa in a microwave for about 1 minute, until lightly warmed (close to room temperature). Add to a large mixing bowl, stirring to break it up.
Drain broccoli and add to the bowl. Chop any large pieces into smaller, bite-sized pieces.
Coarsely chop almonds and add to the bowl.
Chop scallions and parsley and add to quinoa.
Chop figs into small pieces and add to the mixture.
Season with olive oil and lemon juice.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Finish with nutritional yeast and enjoy!
recipes.summary: Crush your vegan macros with this easy grain salad recipe. It's crunchy, vibrant, a little sweet, and somewhat cheesy - and more than enough to keep you feeling satisfied.

recipes.title: Homemade Vegetarian Chili Recipe
ingredients: 1/2 cup roasted red pepper, drained from jar
1 can kidney beans, drained and rinsed
4 tablespoons orange juice or apple cider vinegar
instructions: In a saucepot on medium-high heat, add avocado oil or olive oil, minced garlic, diced onion, celery, and carrot. Stirring occasionally cook for 3 to 5 minutes or until the onions begin to brown.

Once the meat and vegetables have browned, add in the spices and roasted red peppers and cook for 1 minute until aromatic and stir to combine. Stir in the crumbled Trifecta meat patties and cook for 3 minutes or add 16 ounces of beyond meat and cook for 5 minutes.

Continue to cook on medium-high heat and add vegetable stock to release any of the brown bits stuck to the pan, then stir in the diced tomatoes and beans; bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer cook for 10 minutes. Season with orange juice or apple cider vinegar, a pinch of salt or pepper is desired then dig in!
recipes.summary: A delicious high protein, one-pot vegetarian chili recipe made with beyond meat burgers and simple ingredients simmered in hearty blend of seasonings.

recipes.title: 40 Healthy Low-Calorie Snacks for Weight Loss to Satisfy Any Craving
instructions: 0.5 grams of fat
3.5 grams of fat
1.5 grams of protein
8.5 grams of protein
4.5 grams of fat
recipes.summary: Get ahead of mindless eating and unhealthy food cravings with easy healthy low-calorie snack options for weight loss, all under 150 caloires each.

recipes.title: High-Protein Vegan Burrito Bowl Recipe
ingredients: 1 can black beans, drained and rinsed from canned
1 red or yellow bell pepper, sliced
Store bought pico de gallo or your favorite salsa
instructions: To make the slaw, combine the shredded red cabbage, carrots, chia seed, lime juice, cilantro, and apple cider vinegar; massage throughly to tenderize the cabbage and then set aside to let marinade while preparing the other ingredients.

To prepare the cauliflower rice, purchase frozen or fresh pre-prepared cauliflower rice and microwave for 2-3 minutes to cook. Then combine with chopped cilantro, lime juice, cumin, a pinch of salt and any other herbs and spices you'd like!
gather and prepare all of your other garnishes and toppings

Prepare the taco filling or in a frying pan on medium heat. saute the beyond meat or trifecta beyond burgers and season with smoked paprika, cumin, and salt and pepper, to taste. Sauté for 6 to 8 minutes and add in 2-3 tablespoons of your favorite salsa or pico de gallo
Portion out as desired and reheat in the microwave for 2 to 3 minutes.
recipes.summary: Packed with 30 grams of protein, cilantro cauliflower rice, and cabbage slaw, you'll love this quick high-protein vegan burrito bowl recipe.

recipes.title: Simple Salmon Salad Recipe
instructions: Mince shallot and zest lemon.
In a small bowl mix lemon zest, lemon juice, shallot, and dijon mustard. Whisk in olive oil until smooth. Season with salt and pepper to taste and set aside.
Wash and chop lettuce and tomatoes. Set aside.
Cook bacon until crispy and let cool. Once cooled, chop into bite-sized pieces.
Peel and cube avocado.
Divide ingredients into four portions and toss together lettuce, tomato, bacon, and avocado.
Pan sear or warm salmon in the oven for a few minutes.
Toss salad greens in dressing and top with warmed salmon.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Perfect your keto or paleo meal prep in minutes with this easy and healthy salmon salad recipe. Get 30 grams of protein and only 7 grams of net carbs, with a ton of flavor and nutrition.

recipes.title: Paleo Curried Chicken Salad Recipe
ingredients: Portion out this salad into eight high protein snacks for the week or scoop out a larger portion and serve for lunch or dinner with the following h
Brown Rice or rice crackers to keep it paleo
A crisp side salad of mixed greens and veggies tossed in a light vinaigrette
Full of flavor and spice without ruining your macros. This super easy twist on a traditional chicken salad is sure to liven up your lunch or snack options this week!
instructions: Chop Trifecta chicken into bite-sized pieces and add to a large mixing bowl.
Chop scallions and add to bowl.
Add carrots and raisins.
In a separate small bowl, toss together mayo, apricot preserves, vinegar, and curry powder. Stir until evenly blended.
Season with salt and pepper.
Enjoy!
recipes.summary: Spicy, sweet and easy to make paleo curried chicken salad. This twist on a traditional chicken salad recipe is sure to liven up your lunch or snack options this week!

recipes.title: Mediterranean Chicken Platter Recipe
instructions: De-stem and finely chop the parsley, dill, scallion, and mint. Set aside 1 tablespoon of dill for the yogurt dressing.
Finely mince 2 tablespoons of cucumber and set aside for the yogurt dressing.
Dice remaining cucumber and tomato.
Mince garlic. Set aside 1 teaspoon for yogurt dressing.

In a small bowl, combine yogurt, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon garlic, 2 tablespoons cucumber, 1 tablespoon dill, and a pinch of salt. Portion into 4 containers and place in the fridge to chill.
In a mixing bowl, toss parsley, mint, scallion, tomato, hemp seeds, and remaining cucumber, garlic, and dill. Add 1 tablespoon of lemon juice, 1 tablespoon olive oil, and a pinch of salt. Set aside.
Warm chicken and slice.
Plate dish starting with tabbouleh and 2 tablespoons of hummus. Then top with chicken and serve with yogurt dressing!
recipes.summary: Easy chicken meal prep so good it will have you feeling like your eating lunch by the Mediterranean sea, sun on your face, watching sailboats float on by. Make this recipe paleo, keto, low carb, low fat with simple swaps!

recipes.title: 30-Minute Vegan Thai Curry Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups bok choy, chopped into pieces
instructions: Mise en place your ingredients and mince or chop any vegetables. If you have purchased pre-baked or enjoy the raw firm tofu, cube it into 1-inch cubes and set aside with the rest of your ingredients or air-fry your tofu separately.

Heat a stockpot or cast-iron pot over medium heat for one minute and add coconut oil. Add in the onions and saute for 1 minute, then add the garlic and ginger along with any other aromatic ingredients. Cook the aromatics for 2 minutes until they are fragrant and translucent.
Add the mushrooms, red bell pepper, and bok choy to the pot and cook for 5 minutes until they are slightly tender.
Add in your coconut milk and broth, being sure to gently release any curry paste or spices stuck to the bottom or sides of the pan.
Bring to a boil then reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes then finish by adding in the tofu or vegan protein of your choice.
recipes.summary: In 30 minutes prepare this vegan Thai curry recipe overflowing with nutrient-dense vegetables coated in a savory red curry coconut broth.

recipes.title: Vegan Pot Pie Recipe
ingredients: 2 sheets of store-bought pie crust or vegan puff pastry
1.5 cups raw mushrooms, diced or 1 cup
instructions: Preheat your oven to 425 degrees and cut your carrots, parsnips, and celery root into medium-sized pieces, about ½” x ½”, mix with 1 tablespoon of oil and any dried spices you’d like (rosemary and thyme), and spread out onto a baking sheet. Roast for 20 minutes until lightly browned and slightly tender.
Prepare the pie crust accordingly to the instructions on the box for a 9-inch pie pan; set aside.

Heat a medium-sized pot over medium heat and add the tempeh, celery, onions, garlic, and mushrooms. Add small amounts of the vegetable stock to saute the ingredients and prevent anything from sticking to the bottom of the pan as needed until the ingredients are slightly browned about 8 minutes.

Once your aromatics and tempeh are slightly browned add in the roasted root vegetables, apple cider vinegar, rosemary, thyme, and tahini. Cook for 1-2 minutes. Add in the vegetable stock and bring to a simmer for 5 minutes. For a creamier filling feel free to use ½ cup more of the stock.

Prepare your slurry by whisking together the cornstarch and some of the liquid from the pot or warm water. Then slowly pour the slurry into your pot and stir to thicken, after 1 minute and then off the heat and continue to stir.
Transfer your vegan pot pie filling to a pie dish or baking dish lined with the cold pie crust or non-stick spray. Then top with another layer of pie dough.

Transfer your hearty pot pie to the oven and bake for 25 to 30 minutes until golden brown, this is more to cook the crust of the pie than the filling. Remove from the oven and let stand before serving, 5 to 7 minutes before you dig in!
recipes.summary: In less than an hour dig into this hearty and cozy vegan pot pie recipe prepared with a creamy and flavorful filling enclosed by a warm, flaky pie crust.

recipes.title: Salmon Grain Bowl Recipe
instructions: Finely chop the parsley and set aside.
Zest and juice the lemon and set aside.
Coarsely chop scallion and kale and set aside.
Heat 1/2 tablespoon oil in a hot skillet. Add scallion and cook until browned on one side (5 minutes). Then toss to cook further for 1 to 2 minutes and remove from heat.

Add kale to the hot pan and cook for 1 to 2 minutes, tossing frequently to prevent burning. Add 2 tablespoons of water and continue to cook for 5 to 10 minutes, until soft and cooked through. Remove from heat and add to bowl with scallions.
Toast pistachios in a dry skillet for 2 to 3 minutes until warmed through. Set aside.
Warm brown rice in the microwave and add to a large mixing bowl.
Toss rice with scallion, kale, parsley, and lemon zest.
Add 1/2 tablespoon of oil to skillet and cook salmon for 1 to 2 minutes per side, until browned but not cooked through.
Toss lemon juice, remaining oil, salt, and pistachios into rice mixture. Portion into 4 containers and top with salmon.
recipes.summary: The perfect blend of zesty, salty, crunch and fresh flavors paired with savory salmon. Make ahead to enjoy this super easy meal prep recipe any day of the week, or wow your dinner guests without breaking a sweat!

recipes.title: Protein Chicken and Waffles Recipe
instructions: Pre-heat waffle iron and cover in non-stick cooking spray.
In a bowl, whisk together waffle mix, seasoning, egg, and milk, until blended.
Dip chicken breast in the batter to form a thick coating and add to the air fryer. Cook for 10 to 12 minutes at 360 degrees Fahrenheit.
Add remaining batter to waffle iron and cook according to instructions.
Serve warm with your favorite toppings!
recipes.summary: Protein waffles and air fried chicken meet to create this easy healthy breakfast. Use this recipe to impress your friends or to just eat breakfast like a boss. With 44 grams of protein per serving and less than 400 calories a serving, is there even a better way to start your day?

recipes.title: 20 Healthy Super Bowl Snacks that Won't Blow Up Your Macros
instructions: 1. Peanuts, Almonds, & Other Nuts
2. Popcorn
3. Grass-fed Jerky
4. Trail Mix
5. String cheese
6. Shrimp Cocktail
7. Pickles and Olives
8. Veggies and Hummus or Guacamole
9. Whole Grain Tortilla Chips and Salsa
10. Deli Meat Roll-ups and Cheese
10.5 grams of fat, 25 grams of protein, and 10 grams of net carbs.
recipes.summary: Here’s your quick guide for tasty, macro balanced healthy superbowl snack ideas and recipes that will still be crowd-pleasers on game day.

recipes.title: Healthy Salmon Chowder Recipe
ingredients: 1/4 cup chopped cilantro leaves and stems
1 to 2 chipotle peppers in adobo, diced
instructions: Heat large pot on the stove over med-high heat.
Heat oil, chopped onion, cilantro stems and saute until starting to brown, 3 to 4 minutes.
Add chipotle peppers and cauliflower. Cook for 1 to 2 minutes.
Add chicken broth and bring the pot to a simmer.
Let cook for 5 minutes, or until cauliflower is tender.
Over medium-high heat, add spinach and corn. Let cook for 1 to 2 minutes.
Crumble salmon to bite-size chunks and add to the pot. Reduce heat to a gentle simmer.
Remove from heat and stir in Greek yogurt.
Serve warm with finishing salt and chili flakes to taste!
recipes.summary: Step up your meal prep game with this light and smoky salmon chowder. Made with wild caught salmon, chipotle peppers, fresh spinach, corn, and cauliflower and seasoned to perfection. Greek yogurt adds a creamy finish and extra protein. Make this flavor-packed recipe in less than 20 minutes!

recipes.title: Keto Green Bean Casserole
instructions: Blanch green beans for 2 to 3 minutes, as explained in the description above.
Cook bacon in the oven at 425F until crispy tender, on a foil-lined sheet tray. Cooldown and rough chop. Keep aside.
Mix slivered almonds and parm in a food processor, pulsing two to three times until fully incorporated. Set aside in a small bowl.

Dice shallots. Mix cream cheese, shallots, dijon, heavy cream, Worcestershire sauce, and tamari in a food processor. Season with salt and pepper. Make sure the mix is a running thick consistency. Adjust thickness as needed with water or additional cream.
In a large bowl, mix green beans and cream cheese mix. Add bacon and mix to incorporate.
Place a green bean mix in an oven-safe baking dish. Sprinkle almond/parm mix on top.
Bake in oven for 10 min. Change oven to broil setting at high, and broil until top of is golden brown.
recipes.summary: Keto Green Bean Casserole is a perfect side throughout the year, but also a perfect side for your holiday meals, like Thanksgiving and/or Christmas. Be sure to take your sides to any party you attend so that you can also enjoy all the celebration and sharing with your loved ones while staying keto!

recipes.title: Keto Gravy
ingredients: 80 fl oz beef or veg stock, or bone broth
0.5 cup/150 mL Coconut Cream, Rich & Extra Thick
instructions: In a saucepan, combine broth, heavy cream, butter, Tabasco, Tamari, and bay leaves. Bring to a gentle simmer and stir often, incorporating the butter as it melts.
Simmer for 10 - 15 minutes, until sauce slightly reduces.

Add black pepper and mix. While whisking fast, add Xanthan gum to avoid lumps from forming, 1 tsp. At a time. I would suggest you use a hand blender for this, as Xanthan gum is activated with high power blending.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
recipes.summary: This keto gravy sauce can be used for throughout the year, but also it's the perfect rich gravy compliment for any holiday meal, from thanksgiving, to Christmas and even new years. Have fun with it!

recipes.title: Keto Cauliflower Mac and Cheese Recipe
ingredients: 6.5 cups cauliflower, cut into very small florets
1/4 cup extra sharp cheese, whole, shredded
1/8 cup Monterey Jack cheese, whole, shredded
Kosher salt and pepper, to taste
1/8 teaspoon cayenne pepper, if desired, for a kicker
Step One: Prep the Cauliflower and Season It

Start by prepping your cauliflower. Look for a large head of cauliflower with no dark spots. Trim away the leaves and cut them into small cauliflower florets, enough to imitate the size of macaroni pasta. You can also include small pieces of stem.

Add your cauliflower pieces to a large bowl and toss with garlic powder, salt, black pepper, and a drizzle of oil. Don’t forget to save some pepper and garlic powder for the cheese mixture. Then toss gently to coat.

Place cauliflower in a foil-lined sheet tray and bake in a preheated oven, for 12-15 minutes, until tender and roasted. Remove once golden brown and cooked through. Check constantly to avoid florets from burning.
As your cauliflower roasts, you can crisp up your bacon. If you're trying to keep this dish vegetarian, just skip this step.
Cut bacon strips half lengthwise, and then width-wise to small rectangular strips. It might help to stack your bacon for cutting and be sure to use a sharp knife.

Put your bacon pieces in an unheated sauté pan, and bring the heat up from cold to medium - this allows the bacon to begin rendering and cook slowly. Let the pieces sit and don't stir until the bacon begins to crisp up.

Once the bacon begins looking like crispy small teeth, stir with a wooden utensil, and remove from heat once fully crispy. Once done, transfer the bacon to a dish with paper towels to drain excess fat. Keep on the side until needed.
Once the cheese is shredded, add your cheese mixture into a medium saucepan with the cream, nutritional yeast, and a pinch of black pepper, and cayenne pepper (if using).

Bring heat up to medium-low until cheese begins to melt, and make sure you are constantly stirring/whisking to prevent clumping or sticking. It helps if you bring up the heat slowly and stand next to the pot to keep an eye on it.
Once fully melted, reduce heat to low and keep warm, stirring every once in a while until ready. If the mixture is too thick, add extra milk or heavy cream.

Substitute cheese with nutritional yeast, and butter for ghee if vegan or lactose-intolerant. Nutritional yeast will also boost B-vitamins and ghee provides a delicious flavor (the caveat is that it is a saturated fat - limit the use of such).
Step Four: Mix Roasted Cauliflower with Cheese Sauce

In a large bowl (you can use the same bowl you seasoned your cauliflower in at the beginning), mix roasted cauliflower, bacon, and cheese sauce, making sure each piece of cauliflower is evenly coated.

Pour mixture into an oven-safe dish and top off with extra cheese. Broil on high for a minute or two or until cheese begins to brown. Remove from oven and serve immediately. Feel free to add chives or green onion bulbs for garnish.
Looking for Keto Meal Prep? We Got You!
like this? Learn how to hit your
with ease with this free meal prep toolkit for keto. A step-by-step guide on how to plan amazing keto dishes for weight loss.

The secret for this perfect side dish is making the cheese sauce from scratch and roasting the cauliflower. Serve this keto cauliflower mac and cheese recipe for dinner or as a larger family-style meal during any holiday.
6.5 cups cauliflower, cut into very small florets
Kosher salt and freshly ground pepper, to taste
1/8 teaspoon cayenne pepper, if desired, for a kicker
instructions: Start by prepping your cauliflower. Look for a large head of cauliflower with no dark spots. Trim away the leaves and cut them into small cauliflower florets, enough to imitate the size of macaroni pasta. You can also include small pieces of stem. Add your cauliflower pieces to a large bowl and toss with garlic powder, salt, black pepper, and a drizzle of oil. Don’t forget to save some pepper and garlic powder for the cheese mixture. Then toss gently to coat.

Place cauliflower in a foil-lined sheet tray and bake in a preheated oven, for 12-15 minutes, until tender and roasted. Remove once golden brown and cook through. Check constantly to avoid florets from burning. As your cauliflower roasts, you can crisp up your bacon. If you're trying to keep this dish vegetarian, just skip this step.Cut bacon strips half lengthwise, and then width-wise to small rectangular strips. It might help to stack your bacon for cutting, and be sure to use a sharp knife. Put your bacon pieces in an unheated sauté pan, and bring the heat up from cold to medium - this allows the bacon to begin rendering and cook slowly. Let the pieces sit and don't stir until the bacon begins to crisp up.Once the bacon begins looking like crispy small teeth, stir with a wooden utensil, and remove from heat once fully crispy. Keep aside in a plate lined with paper towels to absorb the excess fat. Bonus flavor alert! Feel free to use some of the fat and stir it into the cheese sauce (this will add extra flavor).

Once cheese is shredded, add your cheese mixture (minus a little you'll save for topping off your mac in the final step) into a medium saucepan with the cream, nutritional yeast and remaining black pepper and garlic powder. Bring heat up until cheese begins to melt, and make sure you are constantly stirring/whisking to prevent clumping or sticking. It helps if you bring up the heat slowly and stand next to the pot to keep an eye on it. Once fully melted, reduce heat to low and keep warm, stirring every once in a while until ready. If mixture is too thick, add extra milk or heavy cream.

In a large bowl (you can use the same bowl you seasoned your cauliflower in at the beginning), mix roasted cauliflower, bacon and cheese sauce, making sure each piece of cauliflower is evenly coated. Pour mixture into oven safe dish and top off with remaining cheese. Broil on high for a minute or two or until cheese begins to brown. Remove from oven and serve immediately. Feel free to add chives or green onion bulbs for garnish.
recipes.summary: Find an incredibly delicious and simple keto cauliflower mac and cheese recipe that you won't be able to stay away from! Meal prep ahead of time and enjoy!

recipes.title: Keto Mashed Cauliflower
ingredients: 6.5 cups/800g Cauliflower, Cut into Very Small Florets
0.5 cup/56.5 g Cheddar Cheese, Shredded + 0.25 cup/28.25 g to top off and broil
instructions: Preheat oven to 420° F.

Cut cauliflower to very small floret size, enough to imitate the size/texture of macaroni pasta. Place in a foil lined sheet tray and drizzle with garlic powder, salt, 2 Tsp. pepper, and oil; toss gently to coat. Bake, for 12-15 minutes, until tender. Check constantly to avoid florets from burning. Remove once golden brown.

Cut bacon strips half lengthwise, and then widthwise to small rectangular strips. Add bacon to a sauté pan, and bring the heat up from cold to medium. The bacon will begin rendering and cooking slowly. Don't stir for some time, until bacon begins to crisp up. Once the bacon begins looking like crispy small teeth, stir with a wooden utensil, and remove from heat once fully crispy. Keep aside in a plate lined with paper towels. This will absorb the excess fat. Feel free to use some of the fat and stir it into the cheese sauce (this will add extra flavor).

In a medium saucepan, combine cream, nutritional yeast, cheese, and remaining 2 Tsp. black pepper and 2 Tsp. of garlic powder. Over medium heat, and constantly stirring, heat up mixture while the cheese melts and incorporates. Mix often with a whisk. Once fully melted, reduce heat to low and keep warm, stirring every once in a while until ready. If the mixture is too thick, add extra milk.

Switch oven to broil. Once cauliflower is ready, transfer to a baking dish or a shallow oven-safe baking dish, enough to hold the cauliflower and the sauce together. Combine the former with the bacon, and the cheese sauce. Add 1/4 cup of cheese on top and broil until cheese is golden brown and crispy on top. Feel free to add chives or green onion bulbs for garnish. Serve warm.
recipes.summary: Keto Mashed Cauliflower is a perfect side dish throughout the year, but also a great side for your holiday meals, like Thanksgiving and/or Christmas. Take your cauliflower sides to any event so that you can also enjoy all the celebration and sharing with your loved ones while staying keto!

recipes.title: Keto Chocolate Pudding
ingredients: 2.7 fl oz./80 ml coconut milk, full fat
instructions: In a bowl, combine the coconut milk, cocoa powder, and erythritol. Mix with an electronic mixer until all ingredients are combined, and no large lumps are visible.

You can also do the first step on a food processor to reduce the number of dishes being used or feel free to use a hand blender to incorporate all ingredients in a bowl. A food processor would make the job much easier, but feel free to use what you have available.
